column2: Tyler Silvernail] Hi Tyler, I believe I recall that you said we could apply outstanding credits in the cycle update process at any time. Is that correct? Typically, the only time we do that is during our district billing cycle. I’m looking at account #10801940. The owner made two identical payments, one of which generated a credit balance. When we reversed one of the payments the payment that got reversed was the payment that cleared the bucket items (water, water infra…..) not the “credits” transaction. Now there is an amount in the Credit bucket and balances on the water. I’d like to apply that credit now so it doesn’t charge interest and penalties when we apply them. Will that take care of it, or is there something else I would need to do. Or, do we have to wait until the cycle update when we bill district #1 again? Cindy Albertine Clerk of the Water Board Gloversville Water Works (518)773-4520 ext 1004 calbertine@gloversvillewater.com alber

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Cindy, This account is fine. Since it was a payment and reverse payment; the system knows how to handle that accordingly. Where you guys have issues is direct CR and DR adjustments. The direct CR (water as an example) would ONLY credit water. Payments don't do that, they are applied to charges based on timing of the transaction (oldest first) then by Charge item (as configured in your system). Not by specific charge calcs. This account looks okay, its back to a 0 balance. T
Tyler Silvernail] No worries, Let me know if you need anything else! T"

Susan Thomas] Hi Tammy, Please follow the steps below to void the existing ACH file, create a new one with the correct date, and then re‑import the payments: Void the incorrect ACH file in the system to ensure it does not get processed. Generate a new ACH file using the correct effective date. To void unwanted payments in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, follow these steps: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the correct Posting Date for the adjusting entries (this date will be used for both Accounts Receivable and General Ledger transactions) Click Adjust Select the customer whose payment you want to void Go to the Payments tab In the Payment Selection Grid, click on the row containing the payment you wish to void (the selected payment will be highlighted) You can identify payments by their PayDate, RefNr, Amount, PayMethod, BatchNr, and Comment Click the Void button at the bottom right (only click ONCE) The selected payment will appear in the Adjustments section at the bottom of the screen Click Save to complete the void process Important Notes: Only click the Void button once to avoid the ""Records already exist"" error message Voiding a payment generates reversing entries that cancel out the original amount If Accounts Receivable is interfaced with General Ledger, reversing entries will also be posted to General Ledger This process should be used when a check does not clear the bank or when a payment is posted to an incorrect customer

Susan Thomas] Dear TAMMY, The resolution for this ticket has been provided. If the issue has not been resolved please update the ticket as soon as possible by responding in this ticket. (Please note: You have up to 7 days to notify inHANCE of any issues or concerns related to this ticket. If no issues or concerns have been noted within the 7-day window, the issue is considered complete and this ticket will automatically close.) However, if the issue has been resolved, please close the ticket and take a moment to provide your rating of the service you received.

When I started working here a few years ago I know that Mitch was able to fix this same issue behind the scenes in a matter of minutes. Can this be done like it always was in the past?

Mitch Mullins] The posting date now comes from the ACH file itself ever since we added the ability to include multiple withdraw dates in a single ACH file. I'd recommend Kimberly review a customer history report or customer lookup history for one of these customers to confirm the actual date used for the payment. If we confirm the wrong date was used we can work out the SQL statements that would be used to change the date.

Susan Thomas] 1648320 1649078

Mitch Mullins] The PaymentKey is actually more useful than UBTransactions.Key. This is because we also need to delete payment records from UBBudgetTrans and UBPayments. We can confirm the total payment amount with the following query. Select Sum(Amount) From UBTransactions Where PaymentKey Between 100506 and 100719 Once we've confirmed the payment total we can update payment dates with the following SQL statements. Update UBTransactions Set PostingDate = '3/17/2026' Where PaymentKey Between 100506 and 100719; Update UBBudgetTrans Set ""Date"" = '3/17/2026' Where PaymentKey Between 100506 and 100719; Update UBPayments Set PayDate = '3/17/2026' Where ""Key"" Between 100506 and 100719; Since the GLTransKey values in UBTransactions are blank we know these amounts have not yet been posted to GL so nothing to do with GLTrans.

Susan Thomas] I reached out to Kimberly and connected remotely. I ran the SELECT query, and Kimberly verified that the total amount was correct. After confirmation, I executed the UPDATE queries to update the ubtransactions, ubbudgettrans, and ubpayments tables. Kimberly mentioned she will run the reports shortly.

Tricia Kuszak] I need a report that gives me the sewer reading for January and February. Also need a report that gives us the total of water sales

Joe Sheehan] Issue Tricia Kuszak from Loup City (NE) is requesting two specific reports from PM - Utility Billing EZ: (1) a report showing sewer readings for January and February, and (2) a report showing the total of water sales. Proposed Solution Based on similar tickets, PowerManager has built-in reporting tools that can address both of these needs. For the sewer readings for January and February, the Calculate Customer Amounts application (UB > Maintenance > Rate > Calculate Customer Amounts) can be used to generate a report of sewer-related data for a specified date range without necessarily posting any changes. For the total water sales report, the Billing Statistics or Billing Summary Report (UB > Reports > Financial > Summary) can be used to pull water usage and sales totals for a desired date range. Additionally, the Rate Consumption Analysis report may be useful if a water-only breakdown is needed. Steps to Complete For sewer readings (January & February): Navigate to UB > Maintenance > Rate > Calculate Customer Amounts, set the date range to January and February, and run/print the report without posting to review sewer reading data. For total water sales: Navigate to UB > Reports > Financial > Summary and pull the Billing Summary Report for the desired date range to obtain water sales totals. If a more specific water-only usage breakdown is needed, consider running the Rate Consumption Analysis report, which filters results by water rates and usages billed. If further customization is needed, the Report Designer tool can be used to create a user-defined report tailored to the customer's specific requirements. BRANDI KLOEPPING] I have auditors asking me why I am doubling up on Sanitation and possibly the cash account, I don't know how one balance is going in automatically, but I know the GL is going in by me entering it every month so I need someone to show me where to see the other side of an entry.

BRANDI KLOEPPING] I have auditors asking me why I am doubling up on Sanitation and possibly the cash account, I don't know how one balance is going in automatically, but I know the GL is going in by me entering it every month so I need someone to show me where to see the other side of an entry.

Tony Williams] Possible Fix??? Issue Brandi Kloepping from the City of Cozad is reporting that auditors have flagged what appears to be a duplicate entry for Sanitation charges in the GL. It seems that one side of the entry is being posted automatically by the Utility Billing system (likely via an offset/AR account configured in the UB rate code setup), while Brandi is also manually entering the GL journal entry each month — resulting in a doubled balance on the Sanitation and possibly the cash account. Proposed Solution The root cause is most likely that the Sanitation rate code in Utility Billing has a GL offset account configured, which automatically posts entries to the General Ledger when billing is processed. Because Brandi is also manually entering a General Journal entry for the same transaction each month, the amounts are being recorded twice. We need to identify where the automatic GL posting is originating from within the UB rate code setup, review the offset account configuration, and determine whether the manual journal entry should be discontinued or if the automatic posting needs to be adjusted or disabled. Additionally, it should be verified that the Sanitation billing rate is not listed more than once on affected customer accounts, as duplicate billing rates have been a known cause of doubled charges in similar cases. Steps to Complete Connect remotely with Brandi to review the Utility Billing rate code setup for Sanitation and identify the GL offset account that is configured there. Navigate to the Sanitation rate code in UB Maintenance and check the GL account(s) assigned to it — this is where the automatic posting originates. Review the General Ledger transaction history for the Sanitation and cash accounts to confirm the duplicate entries and identify which source (UB automatic vs. manual GJ) is creating each entry. Check affected customer accounts in the Billing Rates tab to confirm that Sanitation is not listed more than once. If duplicates exist and cannot be deleted (due to existing transactions), set the duplicate rate to ""Inactive."" Once the automatic GL posting is confirmed, advise Brandi to discontinue the manual General Journal entry for Sanitation going forward, as the UB module is already handling that side of the entry automatically. If any prior months have doubled entries, work with Brandi to make a correcting General Journal entry to reverse the duplicate postings. Validate the corrections by reviewing the GL account balances and confirming the entries are no longer doubled.

Susan Thomas] Hi Vicky, Please follow the directions below. If you need any further assistance, feel free to reach out. Option 1: Using the Add-On Amount Field (Recommended Method) Navigate to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts Find and select the specific customer you want to set a custom rate for Click Edit to open their account details Go to the Billing-Rates tab Select the rate you want to customize for this customer Modify the Add-On Amount field to the desired value This will add or subtract from the standard rate amount Use a positive value to increase the rate Use a negative value to provide a discount Option 2: Creating a Special Rate Code If the customer needs a completely different rate structure: Navigate to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Rate Click Add to create a new rate Set up the rate with the desired components and pricing Give it a descriptive name (e.g., ""Special Water Rate - Customer Name"") Once created, assign this rate to the specific customer: Go to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts Find and select the customer Remove the standard rate if necessary Add the new special rate you created Important Notes: The Add-On Amount method is simpler and maintains consistency with your standard rates Creating a special rate code gives you more flexibility but requires more maintenance Any rate changes to standard rates won't automatically apply to custom rates Document any custom rates for future reference and billing verification

Mitch Mullins] The reprint option for AP check printing may be used to re-number checks. Reprinting will ask for the starting and ending check number to be re-printed (the incorrect check numbers) and the correct starting check number. Checks may be printed to paper or a pdf file.

Sreya Johny] Lori, You can update the system to match the paper checks that were already mailed to vendors by using the reprint option in AP check printing. This will allow you to re-number the checks in your system. Reprinting will ask for the starting and ending check number to be re-printed (the incorrect check numbers) and the correct starting check number. Go to AP > Processing > Pay Invoices Enter the original Payment Date and select the vendor account Enter the incorrect check number in the Starting Check Number to Reprint and Ending Check Number to Reprint fields Enter the correct starting check number in the First New Check Number fields and click Next You can print these to either paper or a PDF file. This process will update your system to reflect the correct check numbers that were sent to your vendors. Let me know if you have any questions."

Tricia Kuszak] I am working on the payroll and the report does not match the hours of the employees. I need to get this fixed as soon as possible so the payroll can go to the bank.

Susan Thomas] Recommended Steps First, verify the posted hours by running the Hours report: Navigate to Payroll > Reports > Employee > Hours Enter the date range for the current pay period Select all affected employees This report will show all posted hours detail and help identify discrepancies If discrepancies are found, check the following: Ensure all hours were posted correctly in Post Hours Verify the correct date was used when posting hours Check if any hours were distributed to incorrect GL accounts To correct any errors: Go to Payroll > Processing > Post Hours Select the affected employee(s) Review and correct any incorrectly posted hours Make sure the total hours in Posted Hours - Detail equals the hours in Posted Hours - Master After corrections, recalculate the pay period amounts: Go to Payroll > Processing > Calculate Enter the pay period ending date and check date Click Calculate and select the affected employees Verify the corrections by running the Journal report: Go to Payroll > Reports > Financial > Journal Check the ""Current Pay Period"" box This report will show earning, deduction, and net pay totals for each employee Once verified, you can proceed with printing paychecks or processing direct deposits.

Michelle Roach] When I do late fees every month it is posting a late fee to way past due accounts from years ago. Is there something that can be done to stop this?

Susan Thomas] Solution for Preventing Late Fees on Old Accounts When running your monthly late fee process, you can use the following options to prevent penalties from being applied to very old accounts: Option 1: Use the Penalties & Fines Setup Form to Configure Date Thresholds Navigate to UB > Processing > Penalties & Fines Click the Setup button In the Percentages tab: Adjust the ""Days prior to due date considered current"" setting Set appropriate percentages for Current and Arrears balances For very old accounts, consider setting the Arrears percentage to 0% Option 2: Use Customer Selection Criteria During Penalty Calculation Navigate to UB > Processing > Penalties & Fines Enter your Bill Due Date and Posting Date Click Calculate When the customer selection form appears, use the filtering options to exclude older accounts: Filter by specific date ranges Select only customers with recent activity Exclude inactive accounts or accounts with very old balances Option 3: Transfer Credits to Clear Old Balances For accounts with both credits and old balances, use the Transfer Credits function: Navigate to UB > File > Data File Maintenance > Transfer Credits Set up the payment entry screen with a batch number Click CR Transfer and select all customers This will apply any available credits to arrears balances, potentially clearing old accounts from the penalty process Option 4: Mark Old Accounts as Penalty Exempt For specific accounts with very old balances that should never receive penalties: Navigate to UB >Maintenance> Customer Account Select the customer-Customer tab Check the ""Penalty Exempt"" option for these accounts This comprehensive approach should help prevent late fees from being applied to way past due accounts from previous years while maintaining proper penalty application for current accounts.

Bonnie Ralston] When I imported the ACH payments from those who provided bank information, I accidentally chose the wrong date. Is there a way to change this without voiding all payments and reentering them one at a time? Or a way to just change the date of the payment in the account?

Susan Thomas] Hi Bonnie, Please follow the instructions below to void the payments. Unfortunately, there isn't a direct way to simply change the date of ACH payments after they've been imported without voiding them. Void and Re-enter: You would need to void the payments and re-enter them with the correct date. To void unwanted payments in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, follow these steps: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the correct Posting Date for the adjusting entries (this date will be used for both Accounts Receivable and General Ledger transactions) Click Adjust Select the customer whose payment you want to void Go to the Payments tab In the Payment Selection Grid, click on the row containing the payment you wish to void (the selected payment will be highlighted) You can identify payments by their PayDate, RefNr, Amount, PayMethod, BatchNr, and Comment Click the Void button at the bottom right (only click ONCE) The selected payment will appear in the Adjustments section at the bottom of the screen Click Save to complete the void process Important Notes: Only click the Void button once to avoid the ""Records already exist"" error message Voiding a payment generates reversing entries that cancel out the original amount If Accounts Receivable is interfaced with General Ledger, reversing entries will also be posted to General Ledger This process should be used when a check does not clear the bank or when a payment is posted to an incorrect customer

column2: Tristan Plessel] We had a customer put down a deposit for $150 for new utility services on 2/12/26 batch #202 but ended up not needing the services- we now need to reimburse the customer. Not sure how to since the batch has been closed out.

Tristan Plessel] We had a customer put down a deposit for $150 for new utility services on 2/12/26 batch #202 but ended up not needing the services- we now need to reimburse the customer. Not sure how to since the batch has been closed out.

Sreya Johny] Need to review with Mitch: Steps to complete Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Deposit Select the customer account that needs the deposit refunded Click the ""Calculate"" button to determine the deposit amount to be refunded ($150) Click ""Finalize"" to process the refund, which will create an AP invoice Go to Accounts Payable > Enter Payables to verify the invoice was created correctly Process the AP payment through the normal AP workflow (Prepare Payments) to issue a check to the customer If needed, you can edit the AP invoice vendor information before printing the check if the refund needs to go to someone other than the original customer Additional Information If the deposit doesn't appear in the Deposits processing screen, you may need to check the deposit records in the Table Utilities program. Look at the ""ubdeposittrans"" table to ensure the deposit is properly recorded with the correct service type. If there are issues with the deposit record, it may need to be corrected before processing the refund. Mitch Dahl] I think the AI generated steps are basically correct. There is a possibility that this was just entered in Cash Register and the deposit was never put on the customer's account in UB. If that is the case, they would just need to enter a payable through AP manually.

Sreya Johny] Tristan, There are two possible ways to handle this depending on how the deposit was originally recorded in your system. Option 1: If the deposit was recorded in Utility Billing If the deposit was properly recorded in the customer's UB account, follow these steps: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Deposit Select the customer account that needs the deposit refunded Click the Calculate button to determine the deposit amount to be refunded ($150) Select the Type as Refunded Deposit (to generate an Accounts Payable check to the customer for the refund amount) Click Finalize to process the refund, which will create an AP invoice Go to Accounts Payable > Enter Payables to verify the invoice was created correctly Process the AP payment through the normal AP workflow (Prepare Payments and Pay Invoices) to issue a check to the customer Option 2: If the deposit was only entered in Cash Register If the deposit was only recorded in Cash Register and never added to the customer's UB account, you'll need to create a manual AP invoice: Go to Accounts Payable > Enter Payables Create a new invoice for the customer Enter $150 as the invoice amount Use an appropriate expense account for deposit refunds Include a description referencing the original deposit (date 2/12/26, batch #202) Process the payment through the normal AP workflow to issue a check Please let me know if you have any questions or need additional assistance with this process.

Tricia Kuszak] I need to move out a customer and put the account into another name.

Chris Holloway] Issue The customer needs to move out a customer from a property and transfer the account to a new customer name (change property ownership). Proposed Solution To change ownership of a property in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, you need to perform a customer move-out for the current customer and then a move-in for the new customer at the same location. This process will ensure proper billing transition between customers. Steps to complete Create a new Service Order ticket using the ""Ticket with UB Location and Customer data (Move out, Reactivate)"" option to move out the current customer. In the Service Order Edit form, click the ""Move-Out"" button to display the Move Out form. On the Move Out form, decide whether to make the customer inactive immediately or allow them to receive one more bill: If they should receive one more bill, leave ""Make inactive immediately"" unchecked If they should be made inactive right away, check ""Make inactive immediately"" (note: this will delete any current non-finalized charges) Enter the disconnection date (the date the customer moved out) and prorate percentage if needed. Click ""Move"" to complete the move-out process. Create another Service Order ticket using ""Ticket with UB Location data (Move in new customer)"" option. Fill in the new customer's information. Click the ""Move-In"" button to complete the process for the new customer. Important Notes Based on the related tickets, be careful when selecting ""Make inactive immediately"" during move-out, as this can prevent final bills from generating. If you need the final customer to receive a bill, do not check this option. Also, verify that meter readings are properly recorded for both the move-out and move-in to ensure accurate billing. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl Susan Thomas Mitch Mullins Referenced Documents 100080: Billing 91934: Homer - Help renumber accounts and add a new property 107367: Deposit Refund Question 90097: Duplicate Customer 89250: Meter Change Out Help

Susan Thomas] Use Use Customer Accounts Move Out/In to simultaneously close out one account at a location and activate a new account at that location. The Move Out/In form is divided into two sections. The left hand section (Move Out) is used to provide information about the account being closed out and the right hand section (Move In) is used to provide information about the new account. First complete the Move Out side and then the Move In side. UB - Customer Accounts Move Out/In Form UB - Customer Accounts Move Out/In Form Edit/Display Fields ·Move Out - Provide information for the account being closed out. ·Make Inactive Immediately - Indicate if you wish to close the account immediately without producing a final bill. ·Billing Status - After selecting an account for move out, displays where in the billing cycle the account currently stands. ·Cust # - Enter/select the customer account being closed out. ·Name - Displays the name of the customer account selected for move out. ·Rt-Acct - Displays the route book number and location account number of the customer account selected for move out. ·Disconnected - Enter the date of the last day of service for the account being moved out. ·Prorate % - Enter the percentage of the full charge to be billed. ·Addr Lines - Enter the mailing address to which the final bill will be sent. ·Final Readings Grid - Enter the final meter reading(s) and the date of the final meter reading(s). ·Move In - Provide information for the new account. ·Make Active Immediately - Check this option if the new customer should be billed this billing cycle. Leave this option un-checked to create the new customer as pre-active and not to be billed until the next billing cycle. ·Type Of Account - Select as follows: ·Existing Customer - Use this option to bill an existing customer for the move out location. You will select the customer from the Customer Selection form, the existing customer information will be automatically filled in and editing of this information will not be allowed ·New Customer - Use this option to create totally new Customer and Master Address table records for the new account and then enter the customer and mailing address information. · Reactivate - Use this option to activate a customer account that had previously been billed at the move out location. You will choose the inactive account from the Customer Selection form displaying only accounts that had previously been billed at the move out location. The customer information will be automatically filled and editing of this information will not be allowed. ·Owner - Use this option to reactivate the owner of the property selected for the move out location. This requires that the owner has been assigned to the location on the Location page of the Customer Accounts Edit form. The customer information will be automatically filled and editing of this information will not be allowed. ·Cust# - Enter the desired customer number. The highest existing customer number + 1 will be suggested. ·Name - Enter the new customer's name. ·Connected - Enter the date of the first day of service for the account being moved in. ·Addr Lines - Enter the mailing address to which the new bill will be sent. ·Initial Readings Grid - Enter the initial meter reading(s) and the date of the initial meter reading(s). ·Deposits Grid - Enter the date, service type, and amount of any service/meter deposits received for the new account. Optionally enter the deposit receipt number and comment. UB - Customer Accounts Move Out/In Form Buttons ·Cancel - Exit the Customer Accounts Move Out/In form and return to the Customer Accounts form without taking any action. ·Move - Complete the Move Out/In process and clear the form in preparation for processing another Move Out/In.

After doing research I found that the process to print full statements is to print to PDF first, then print the PDF. 1. Run Create forms 2. Spot Check Create forms process 3. Run Print Forms (this will shoot the user back to the Form SQL query) 4. Run SQL Form query with date prompts 5. After done, Press the template builder icon 6. Select the Preview Tab (OR File > Print) 7. Print Icon 8. Print the single PDF document to a file you have access to 9. Once done, navigate to the PDF and print it using your printer of choice. Mitch Dahl] I believe we usually recommend posting a payable through Enter Payables, using the GL cash account(s) as the expense account(s). Then record the check through Pay Invoices. This will get the outstanding check into the system and not affect the cash account(s) balance because the entering of the payable will generate a debit entry and the recording of the check will generate a credit entry.

Sreya Johny] Shelly, For handling outstanding checks from prior to your PM conversion, here's the recommended process: First, post a payable through the Enter Payables function Use the GL cash account(s) as the expense account(s) This will create a debit entry to your cash account Then record the check through Pay Invoices This will generate a credit entry to your cash account This method ensures the outstanding checks are properly entered into your system without affecting your cash account balance, as the debit entry from entering the payable and the credit entry from recording the check will offset each other. Once the checks are in the system this way, you can: Track them for reconciliation purposes Cancel them if needed (either now or in the future) Maintain proper accounting for all outstanding items Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Mitch Dahl] What I usually do in this case is go under Payroll-Processing-Void Paychecks. The current pay period should show up as the first line in the paychecks grid. If it doesn't, and it hasn't been printed, select it and void it (it should just disappear). Then go back and re-post the hours for the employee. It could be something different, so you'll want to take a look to make sure, but this is the most common solution to this problem.

Sreya Johny] Cathy, Please go to Payroll > Processing > Void Paychecks > Select Ashley Halsey > The current pay period should show up as the first line in the paychecks grid. If it doesn't, and it hasn't been printed, select it and void it (it should just disappear) > Return to Post Hours and re-post the hours for that employee > Then try the Calculate function again If you continue to experience issues after following these steps, please let us know and we can provide additional assistance.

Sreya Johny] This issue typically occurs when the current pay period doesn't show up as the first line in the Void Paychecks screen, preventing the system from recognizing posted hours during calculation. To prevent this from happening in the future, follow these steps whenever you encounter calculation errors: Go to Payroll > Processing > Void Paychecks Select the employee(s) experiencing the issue Look for lines where the ""Updated"" column shows ""False"" (these are non-updated lines) If these lines are not at the top of the list, select them and void/delete them. The current pay period should show up as the first line in the paychecks grid. Return to Post Hours and re-post the hours for that employee Try the Calculate function again"

Khili Ferguson] A customer needs to close their account with a balance. How do I do their refund while they still owe a balance?

Tony Williams] I'll help you craft a response to this customer inquiry about refunding a deposit while there's still a balance due. Let me first search our knowledge base for information on this specific issue.Khili, When closing an account with a balance due and a deposit, you have two options: Credit the deposit to the account first, which will apply to the balance due. Then refund only the remaining deposit amount (if any). Have the customer pay the balance due separately, then refund the full deposit. For option 1 (most common): Open the Deposit Register from the customer's account screen Choose to Credit the deposit (this creates a credit in the Other income center) The system will automatically apply this credit to the outstanding balance Process a refund for any remaining deposit amount Remember to only credit back enough deposit to make the balance 0. The rest can be refunded after.

If you send email bills from Impresa and need to use a different email service, you can change that in the Print Bills and Notices process. Sreya Johny] Bonnie, To Remove a Duplicate Deposit from a Customer Account: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the original Posting Date > Adjust Select the customer account with the duplicate deposit Navigate to the Deposits tab In the bottom section labeled Adjustments: Select the appropriate Type Enter a negative amount equal to the duplicate deposit (e.g., if the duplicate is $100, enter -$100) Make sure to match the service in the Adjustments grid with the service to which the original deposit was recorded Click Save to complete the process Please let us know if you have any questions.

How to Modify Automatic Lock Removal Settings: Navigate to UB Administration > Payment Information > Payment Priority In this screen, you'll find options that control when accounts are automatically unlocked: There are typically two options available in this section that function as an ""either/or"" setting One option unlocks accounts when past due balances are paid The other unlocks accounts when some payments are made To disable the automatic removal of locks when an account is paid to zero, you'll need to uncheck these options This configuration allows you to maintain manual control over when customer locks are removed, rather than having the system automatically remove them based on payment status. If you'd like assistance with making these changes or would like to discuss additional options for your specific workflow needs, please let me know and I'd be happy to help further.

column2: Teresa Akeson] We have cut handwritten checks for water deposit refunds, now I need to know how to properly remove the deposit from the account.

Tony Williams] Review for Accuracy -------------------------------------- Teresa, How to Remove Water Deposits After Manual Refund: Navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Deposits Configure the Setup: Click the Setup button Check the Refund Selected Customers checkbox Select the Print a check option (even though you've already issued checks manually) Click Close to save these settings Process the Deposit Removal: Click the Calculate button Select the customer account that received the handwritten refund Choose Refunded Deposit as the Type Enter the refund amount (should match your handwritten check) Click OK Finalize the Transaction: Review the information to ensure it's correct Click the Finalize button Handle the AP Invoice: This will create an AP invoice for the refund Since you've already issued a check, you'll need to void this invoice in Accounts Payable Navigate to Accounts Payable > Processing > Invoices Locate and void the automatically created invoice Alternative Method (If Deposits Option Unavailable): If you don't have access to the Deposits processing option: Navigate to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts Select the customer account Go to the Deposits tab In the Adjustments section: Enter today's date in the Date column Enter a negative amount equal to the deposit (e.g., -$125.00) in the Amount column for the appropriate service Click Save This will remove the deposit from the customer's account to reflect the handwritten refund you've already issued. Let us know if you need any clarification on these steps.

We figure our sewer usage average by using the water reads from Dec, Jan, Feb, and calculate an average for each individual account. I would like assistance on how this works in your system.

Sreya Johny] Teresa, To calculate sewer charge based on the water usage: Go to UB > Maintenance > Rate > Calculate Customer Amounts. Select the Calculation Method as Usage Based and enter the dates based on your water billing (enter 12/1/2025 to 02/28/2026 if calculating based on Dec, Jan, Feb water usage) and click Next. Select the correct Sewer Rate and the Flat Rate Components that need to be used for the calculation and click Next. Click Calculate, select all customers, and click OK. You will then see the new charges reflected in the NewAmt column. Please review the amounts and make any necessary adjustments. You may also print this as a report for future reference. Click Post Amts once you have confirmed that the amounts are correct. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Sreya Johny] Teresa, To calculate sewer charge based on the water usage: Go to UB > Maintenance > Rate > Calculate Customer Amounts. Select the Calculation Method as Usage Based and enter the dates based on your water billing (enter 12/1/2025 to 02/28/2026 if calculating based on Dec, Jan, Feb water usage) and click Next. Select the correct Sewer Rate and the Flat Rate Components that need to be used for the calculation and click Next. Click Calculate, select all customers, and click OK. Click Post Amts once you have confirmed that the amounts are correct. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Sreya Johny] Teresa, Yes, the Calculate Customer Amounts feature will work the way you need. When you select the Usage Based calculation method, the system will: Calculate the average water usage from the specified period (Dec, Jan, Feb) Apply this usage amount as ""sewer usage units"" for each customer Multiply these units by your cost per unit based on your sewer rate components It's specifically designed to calculate sewer charges based on water consumption units. The process will: Take each customer's water usage during the winter months Calculate their individual average usage Apply your per-unit rate to determine their sewer charges When you run this calculation and select your sewer rate, make sure the rate has the appropriate usage-based components configured. After calculation, you'll see the new amounts in the NewAmt column, which will reflect each customer's individual usage-based charges. Please review the charges and correct them if anything does not look right before posting them to the customer accounts.

Tony Williams] Review for accuracy. Issue Sherri has printed payroll checks, but the last two checks did not print initially. When she selected ""No"" on the prompt asking if checks printed correctly and tried to reprint, those two checks did print. However, now these checks are not properly recorded in the system. When attempting to reprint checks with the specific date and check number range, the system is showing all vendors marked for printing instead of just the specific checks that need to be recorded. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, this appears to be an issue where the checks physically printed but were not properly recorded in the PowerManager system. The solution is to use the PR Data File Maintenance option to ""Un-print non-updated Payroll Checks"" and then run through the Print Checks process again to properly record the checks in the system. Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Data File Maintenance Select the option to ""Un-print non-updated Payroll Checks"" This will reset the status of the checks that were printed but not recorded Return to the Print Checks process (Payroll > Process > Print Checks) Go through the Print Checks steps again, entering the correct check number range for just the two checks that need to be recorded When prompted if checks printed correctly, select ""Yes"" to record the checks in the system This will properly record the checks without having to reprint them physically Additional Information This is a common issue that occurs when there's an interruption during the check printing process or when the user indicates checks didn't print correctly. The system allows you to reset and record checks properly without having to repost hours or recreate the entire payroll run. The Data File Maintenance option specifically addresses situations where checks physically printed but weren't properly recorded in the system. Tyler Silvernail] Hi Mike, So in BM, if there are a number of reports that need to be ran - Instead of running them individually each time, what we do is set up a NEW report called a ""Report Chain"" Query. This Report Chain query is written/configured to look at the other reports with prompts/save pathing etc and applies defaults for those values. Then runs all the reports in order for you. So we set task scheduler to look at the chain instead of all the individual reports. I hope this makes sense. If you want to do what the chain does, you would have to open it in view mode and see what report IDs its looking at and run each of them individually. Michael Devenport] Without really knowing what the ""billing"" process does, is this something we can automate so that It can happen after hours like the Daily File task?
Michael Devenport] Without really knowing what the ""billing"" process does, is this something we can automate so that It can happen after hours like the Daily File task?

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Mike, It's not really advisable to automate the billing process. The user needs checks and balances for reporting to ensure proper billing (logs, queries need to be viewed). Mistakes are made and should be caught prior to closing, if all processes are automated then those issues wont be caught. However - Since it's a multistep process, the user could do pre-calculation steps in the evening, then run the calculation update overnight. The user could then come back in the following morning after the update and run reporting to do error checking. So it's not really a matter of automation, its if the user is willing split the timing of each step up to ensure resources are available etc. I hope this makes sense. T

Michelle Nelson] I have an employee that questioned her federal withholding on her W-2. She believes it should be closer to $900 instead of $93. How do I go about making sure that the correct amount was withheld?

Tony Williams] Is this right??? To verify the federal withholding amount on your employee's W-2, you'll need to check several areas in PowerManager. Here's how to confirm whether the correct amount was withheld: Step 1: Review the Employee's W-2/W-4 Information Navigate to Payroll > Maintenance > Employees Select the employee in question and click Edit Go to the W-2/W-4 tab Check the following: Is ""2020 or later W-4"" checked? What filing status is selected? Are there values in any of the W-4 steps (particularly Step 3 credits or Step 4c extra withholding)? Step 2: Verify the Federal Withholding Deduction While still in the employee record, go to the Deductions tab Locate the federal withholding deduction (typically labeled FedM, FedS, etc.) Check if the Compute checkbox is checked Verify the Total amount shown for this deduction If Step 4(c) extra withholding was entered on the W-4, confirm this amount appears in the Extra column Step 3: Run Reports to Confirm Withholding Totals Go to Payroll > Reports > Financial > Journal Uncheck Current Pay Period Set the date range for the entire year (1/1/2025 - 12/31/2025) Select the employee in question Run the report to see all federal withholding amounts for the year Step 4: Check Earnings That Affect Federal Withholding Return to the employee record and go to the Earnings tab Verify that all appropriate earnings are set up correctly For each earning, confirm it's properly configured to be included in W-2 wages Common Reasons for Lower-Than-Expected Withholding: Employee claimed dependents or credits on Step 3 of their W-4 (reduces withholding) Pre-tax deductions lowered the taxable income Employee's W-4 has ""Married"" selected, which uses a higher standard deduction The employee has multiple jobs and checked Step 2 but didn't complete Step 4(c) If you'd like me to help you review this employee's specific setup in more detail, please let me know and we can schedule a call to look at it together.

I need someone at NEA to give me a cutoff date for me to delete statements.. ie - ""keep everything from 2017 and newer"", then I will delete any statement older than 2017. SUE SUDBECK] The register/head on a meter was recently replaced, not the whole meter. It has been a while since this has happened and I was reviewing my notes and want to make sure this is how it should be done. 1. Meter Register Change Out – Only-No New Meter Body (with meter reading) Utility Billing → Processing → Meter Reading → Manual Entry · Default Reading Date → Date Register was Replaced → Click OK · Select Customer · Enter the present reading when the register was replaced · Click Close → Cancel → Close Utility Billing → Maintenance → Customer Accounts → Edit → Select Customer · Click on the Meter Tab · Click the Edit Button under Readings for the new Meter Reading · Check the Billed Box · Click Ok (this reading will disappear from the readings but will be under the readings in the meters tab) · Click on the Edit Button under Meters · Click on the Readings Tab · Edit the Prior Read and Present Read Numbers for the unbilled reading, usually ZERO if it is a new register. · Click Save. **Once Meters have been Read and the readings have been imported into PM go back into Utility Billing → Maintenance → Customer Accounts → Edit → Select Customer · Click on the Meter Tab · Click the Edit Button Under Meters · Click on the Readings Tab · Click on the current billed reading and click Edit · Uncheck Billed, so the gallons will be billed on the statements to be printed.

Tony Williams] Sue, Your procedure for handling meter register change outs is correct. The steps you've outlined match our recommended process for when only the register/head is replaced, not the entire meter body. Confirming Your Meter Register Change Out Procedure: Enter the final reading from the old register: Navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Meter Reading > Manual Entry Set the Reading Date to the date the register was replaced Select the customer and enter the present reading when the register was replaced Click Close > Cancel > Close Mark the reading as billed: Go to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts > Edit > Select Customer Click on the Meter Tab Click the Edit Button under Readings for the new Meter Reading Check the Billed Box Click OK (this reading will disappear from the readings but will be visible under the readings in the meters tab) Update the meter information: Click on the Edit Button under Meters Click on the Readings Tab Edit the Prior Read and Present Read Numbers for the unbilled reading (typically ZERO for a new register) Click Save After new readings are imported: Return to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts > Edit > Select Customer Click on the Meter Tab Click the Edit Button Under Meters Click on the Readings Tab Click on the current billed reading and click Edit Uncheck Billed, so the gallons will be billed on the statements to be printed This approach ensures that the system properly tracks the transition from the old register to the new one, while maintaining accurate consumption calculations for billing purposes. Let me know if you have any other questions about this process.

column1: 21064889,"KBA - How to Save your Database from SQL Server failure","[2026-03-04 09:48
column2: Sachin Yadav] KBA: How to save your Database from major crashes You can follow a few steps to save this Server failure/crash in Impresa: 1. Try restarting the SQL Server on the Services, and then try to test to perform any task, and it should work fine. 2. Sometimes, just after the SQL server migrates to the new machine, we need to run the ""new_one_stop_shop_7.2.sql"" on Oasis, Oasis_master, and msdb. 3. We could make a SQL Job agent named ""sp_inHance_reindex"" that will help the DB to run smoothly. (You may check the timings for other SQL JOB AGENTS that shouldn’t coincide with). 4. We could set the SQL server to use the most current compatibility level, which would be SQL 2019 or SQL 2022. 5. We changed the Server connection properties to maximum server memory to ""12288 MB"". 6. We also made some changes to the Daily BACKUP SQL jobs, such as compressing them more, so that they could save a lot of space on the local drive. 7. Try to contact your IT support to free up some space on the server's local drive. Hopefully, these changes will boost the DB to work faster and without any issues. Please let us know if you still have any questions."

column1: 21064726,"Error Message SQL Server connection spid changed.  Exiting application","[2026-03-04 08:36
column2: Alfredo Moreno] We recently had to make some emergency infrastructure changes in the last week or so. We suspect the error message we are getting is related to us having to house the database on another server. Hopefully a quick fix! Let me know if there's a SQL script or something we can run.

Sachin Yadav] Hi Alfredo, This could happen because of the Sleep settings on this machine, the established network, just losing every time it went to sleep mode. You can follow this KBA to change the settings. How to Fix Error: “SQL server connection spid changed. Exiting application.” Generally when you get the error message “SQL server connection spid changed. Exiting application”, it's received after a workstation goes into sleep mode. This is because the network interface (card/port) had been enabled for shutting down when the workstation sleeps. Because this this function of the network interface turns the power off to the network connection, it is disconnecting the computer from the SQL session. As a result, when the workstation is used again, the network interface comes back up with a new SPID (session number) assigned to the workstation's connection. This is why the workstation shuts down the program and forces a reconnect. How to fix the workstation to not receive this error: 1. Depending on which version of the screen that comes up, this will determine which selection path you choose to take. 1a. If your Control panel looks like the one below:(A) (A) ' 1b . You will want to select ""View network status and task"" (Highlighted in Yellow):(B) (B) 2 . However, if your Control Panel comes up similar to the following:(C) 2a. 2b. You will want to select on ""Network and Sharing Center"" (Highlighted in Yellow):(D) (D) 3. Once you you have selected one of the two displayed selections above, you should see the following screen:(E) (E) 4. Once that screen comes up you will want to select the ""Change Adapter Settings"" from the selection listed on the left hand side of this menu (Highlighted in Yellow):(F) (F) 5. At this point, you should see a similar screen appear on your workstation: (G) (G) 6. Generally, you will need to Right click on the ""Local Area Connection"" (You may need to get your ""IT"" to help on this) and then select ""Properties"" from the drop down menu that should appear:(H) (H) 7. You should now see the following screen:(I) (I) 8. You will want to select the ""Configure"" button at the right hand side of the displayed screen:(J) (J) 9. This will now bring up a screen that looks like the following:(K) (K) 10. At this point you will want to select the ""Power Management"" Tab:(L) (L) 11. This will bring up the screen that allows the power saving feature to be disabled. This feature is generally turned on by default on the workstation. Remove the ""X"" from the ""Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power"".(M) (M) 12. This will bring up the screen that allows the power saving feature to be disabled. This feature is generally turned on by default on the workstation. Remove the ""X"" from the ""Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power"".
Sachin Yadav] Morning Alfredo, Do you still need any assistance on this ticket? Please let us know if you have any questions. Sachin"

Krystal Rutti] Hello, I am working on trying to understand the write-off process. When I go in and get my list and click on F1 to bring up the manual it says that general ledger account numbers to be used are determined by rates created specifically for the write off application, does this mean that we will need to create new rates for electric, water, sewer and recycling to be able to write off the old accounts? Or can I use the current rates that we have? One other question that I have is that when I go into utility billing and pull up the aged accounts receivable report the first account on the list shows that he is carrying a credit in recycling but when I go into customer inquiry it shows that his credit was brought over from our old system (simple city) under electric so I just don't understand why on the aged accounts receivable report it is showing the credit to recycling. Can you explain that to me?

Krystal Rutti] Hello, I am working on trying to understand the write-off process. When I go in and get my list and click on F1 to bring up the manual it says that general ledger account numbers to be used are determined by rates created specifically for the write off application, does this mean that we will need to create new rates for electric, water, sewer and recycling to be able to write off the old accounts? Or can I use the current rates that we have? One other question that I have is that when I go into utility billing and pull up the aged accounts receivable report the first account on the list shows that he is carrying a credit in recycling but when I go into customer inquiry it shows that his credit was brought over from our old system (simple city) under electric so I just don't understand why on the aged accounts receivable report it is showing the credit to recycling. Can you explain that to me?

Mitch Mullins] Typically new rates are added for each type of service to be written off. We do this so we can assign a bad debt or a uncollectable type of expense account. This is preferred instead of reducing the sales revenue account. We would want to review the customer that is showing the credit in the recycling type of service. Not really enough information to respond to this question.

Mitch Mullins] Typically new rates are added for each type of service to be written off. We do this so we can assign a bad debt or a uncollectable type of expense account. This is preferred instead of reducing the sales revenue account. We would want to review the customer that is showing the credit in the recycling type of service. Not really enough information to respond to this question.

Sreya Johny] Krystal, Yes, you will need to create new rates specifically for each type of service (electric, water, sewer, and recycling) that you want to write off. This is the recommended approach because: It allows you to assign appropriate bad debt or uncollectable expense accounts in your General Ledger It keeps your write-offs separate from regular service revenue accounts It provides better financial tracking of written-off amounts by service type Creating these specialized write-off rates ensures proper accounting treatment instead of simply reducing your sales revenue accounts. The discrepancy between where the credit appears (recycling in the aged accounts receivable report vs. electric in customer inquiry) could be due to how the credits were transferred when imported from your old system (Simple City). PowerManager has a ""Transfer Credits"" function that can move credits between service types. It's possible that during the data migration, this credit was inadvertently transferred to recycling while the history record still shows it originated in electric. To investigate this further: Check the customer's transaction history to see if there was a credit transfer transaction Look at the payment history to verify which service type originally received the credit If you'd like us to look at this specific customer account in more detail, please provide a screenshot of the customer history screen and attach the AR report.

SUE SUDBECK] I just finalized February billing, and it showed Errors for the O Route. So I thought I had not computed charges along with print those statements, I was wrong and I had computed the charges I had just not printed the statements, now I have double billing for the O Route and I am unsure how to fix this issue.

Sreya Johny] Sue, You can uncompute the charges once to correct the double charges for the O Route. To uncompute charges: Go to UB > Processing > Compute Charges > Enter the original Posting Date > Click the Setup button > Uncheck all the Billable checkboxes > Save > Check ""Ok to calculate printed bills"" checkbox > Calculate > Select O Route and click OK > Go back to Setup and check all the Billable boxes again. Before printing the bills, please review the affected accounts after the charges have been uncomputed. Please let us know if you have any questions.

column2: Denise DeCoste] We did accounts payable in the general account and the library account. The payments are saying posted on the summary, but we need some checks printed.

Denise DeCoste] We did accounts payable in the general account and the library account. Then printed checks for the general account, went to print the library account and we keep getting an error message that there is ""no vendors."" The payments are saying posted on the summary, but we need some checks printed.

Tony Williams] Denise, I understand you're having trouble printing checks for your Library account. While the payments are showing as posted on the summary, you're getting an error message that there are ""no vendors"" when trying to print the checks. Let me help you resolve this issue. Why This Is Happening This error typically occurs when the payment preparation process hasn't been fully completed for the Library account. Even though the invoices are posted, the system needs to know which vendors to pay and how to pay them. How to Fix the Issue: Verify Payment Preparation: Navigate to Accounts Payable > Processing > Prepare Payments Make sure you're in the Library account (not the General account) Check that invoices have the Pay checkbox selected for the vendors you want to pay If Payments Are Already Prepared: Go to Accounts Payable > Processing > Pay Invoices Select Print Checks as the Payment Method Enter the appropriate Payment Date and click Next On the Vendor Selection screen, click Select All (or select specific vendors) Click OK to proceed Select Bank Account and Check Number: Choose the correct bank account for the Library Enter the starting check number Click Next to continue If You Still Get ""No Vendors"" Error: Return to Prepare Payments and verify that: The correct company/account (Library) is selected There are unpaid invoices visible in the grid The Pay checkbox is checked for invoices you want to pay Click Close to save your changes Check Vendor Setup: Navigate to Accounts Payable > Maintenance > Vendors Verify that the vendors you're trying to pay are set up in the Library account If not, you may need to add them to this account Let me know if you need any clarification on these steps or if you continue to experience issues after trying these solutions.

Susan Thomas] Root Cause Based on the ticket conversation and related documents, the issue appears to be related to missing vendor account numbers or incomplete vendor setup in the Library account. This is preventing the system from properly selecting vendors during the check printing process. Proposed Solution The issue can be resolved by verifying the vendor setup in the Library account and ensuring that all necessary vendor information is complete, particularly the account numbers. As Susan Thomas identified in her earlier message, some vendors have account numbers while others do not. Steps to Complete Verify vendor setup in the Library account by navigating to Accounts Payable > Maintenance > Vendors Check if the vendors you're trying to pay have account numbers assigned in the ""Acct #"" field on the General tab Add missing account numbers to any vendors that don't have them Return to Accounts Payable > Processing > Prepare Payments and verify that: The correct company/account (Library) is selected The Pay checkbox is checked for invoices you want to pay Click Close to save your changes Go to Accounts Payable > Processing > Pay Invoices and follow these steps: Select Print Checks as the Payment Method Enter the appropriate Payment Date and click Next On the Vendor Selection screen, click Select All (or select specific vendors) Click OK to proceed Choose the correct bank account for the Library Enter the starting check number Click Next to continue and complete the check printing process ----Regarding Handwritten Checks---- If you need to handwrite checks temporarily, you should use the ""Record Checks"" option in the Pay Invoices process rather than ""Print Checks."" This will properly record the payment in the system without actually printing the check. Here's how: Go to Accounts Payable > Processing > Pay Invoices Select Record Checks as the Payment Method Enter the Payment Date and click Next Follow the remaining steps to select vendors and complete the process This will ensure the handwritten checks are properly recorded in the system

column1: 21064035,"Customers are being penalized when they have an adjustment with a due date - 00156130102","[2026-03-04 05:33
column2: Jhony Desinor] Issue: Inaccurate penalty assessment on accounts with unbilled adjustmentsDetails: Customers are receiving penalties even when billed balances are paid in full if an unbilled adjustment exists on the account. Account 00156130102 had a $53.00 unbilled adjustment (no due date). Payment for the billed amount was received, but a penalty was still applied.Investigation: Preliminary review suggests the ""pay unbilled balance"" setting in the payment hierarchy may be the cause. While this setting is necessary for other scenarios, it appears to be incorrectly flagging these specific adjustments. We need to refine this logic to exclude adjustments without active due dates from penalty calculations.Request: We need assistance to further investigate the root cause and adjust the logic to prevent automated penalties on unbilled adjustments.JD-3/4/2026

Chris Holloway] Need to either move over down in the payment priority, which we can't do because other adjustments need to be paid before current, or create a new income center and move some of the ""Other"" adjustment codes over to it, so that you can position them where you want.

Chris Holloway] Hi Jhony, To clarify, the adjustment was put on the account from the ""other"" group, which is the first group in the payment priority screen. If you only have a few adjustment codes that you want to be sure gets paid before any other balances, it may make more sense to create a new income center and then create those adjustment codes under that new income center. This would allow you to move the other group lower in the Payment Priority, and put the new income center at the top of the payment priority list. In the screen clippings below, you can see that the payment that was meant for the current charges was used to pay a portion of the new charges and the adjustment.

Jhony Desinor] Vanessa, Harris (Chris) assisted with investigating the issue and provided the following recommendations: Discovery: The investigation shows that the Data Log adjustment falls under the “Other” group, which is positioned at the top of the payment hierarchy in the UBCS Impresa Administration settings. Because of the “pay unbilled balance” configuration, items in this group will always be flagged for payment—even when no active due dates exist. Recommended Solutions: · Option 1 — If only a few adjustment codes need priority payment: If you only have a few adjustment codes that you want to be sure gets paid before any other balances, it may make more sense to: Create a new income center.Move (or migrate) the adjustment codes that must be paid first into this new income center.Place the new income center at the top of the payment priority list.Move the “Other” group lower in the Payment Priority hierarchy. Option 2 — If most adjustments in the “Other” group must remain top priority: Keep the “Other” group at the top of the payment priority list.Create a new income center and move only the selected “Other” adjustment codes into it.Position this new income center where desired within the hierarchy. Implementation strategy: Select one of the solutions above.Test the proposed configuration.If testing is successful, implement the chosen solution in the production environment.. I’ve closed the Harris ticket for now. Please let me know when you’re ready to test these changes for implementation. I can reopen the ticket and coordinate with Harris for further assistance if needed. column1: 21063500,"New Bill Files Exported to Invoice Cloud","[2026-03-04 03:06
column2: TERA EDEN] I need assistance with new bill files and how they are exported to Invoice Cloud. For the last few months, our paperless customers are receiving a notice of a new bill that has a balance of zero, then a few days later will receive another notice showing their new bill amount. I have posted a image of one particular instance, although it is not strictly this customer that is effected, it is all customers.

Susan Thomas] Hi Tera, Please verify whether the bill was fully printed in PowerManager.In Customer Lookup, check the Printed section to confirm that the system recorded the bill as printed. This issue—where paperless customers first receive a zero‑balance notification and then later receive the actual bill amount—usually happens when the bill printing and export process isn’t completed in the correct order. Most often, it’s due to one of the following: Response to Invoice Cloud Export Issue The issue with paperless customers receiving zero balance notifications followed by actual bill amount notifications is likely related to how bills are being processed and exported to Invoice Cloud. This can occur due to two main reasons: Incomplete Bill Printing Process: When bills are generated but the printing process isn't fully completed in PowerManager, the system may not properly record that bills have been printed. This can cause Invoice Cloud to receive incomplete information. Bill Export Sequence: The export process may be sending preliminary bill data to Invoice Cloud before charges are fully computed or finalized. Recommended Solution: Ensure you complete the full bill printing process even for paperless customers: When printing bills, use the ""Print Bills and Create an Export File"" option Complete the entire printing process, which records that bills have been printed in the system For paperless customers, you can print to PDF or use a null printer Make sure to follow the correct sequence: Compute charges completely Print bills (to record them as printed) Export bill files to Invoice Cloud only after both steps are complete Check for duplicate customer numbers in your system, as these can cause issues with Invoice Cloud exports This should prevent the system from sending zero balance notifications followed by actual bill notifications to your paperless customers.

Vicky Goodner] I was told at the beginning of February that you would be looking into my Power Manager update. In January of 2025 I paid $2,743.00 to have my system updated to Enterprise. It is not working right. I have trouble finalizing bills, and when I enter a payment it seems to do fine but when I run the payment report it shows that only water got credited and everyone still owes for sewer and trash. It is time to process the new bills but I don't want the uncredited portion to show up. I asked about updates and if I was missing any. The system showed that it was too old to update. How can this be??? Please help me. Vicky Goodner Village of Dix 1-308-682-5279
Jimmy Biondo] Hi, this is Vicky Goner with the village of Dix.

Mitch Mullins] 1. Use the Transfer Credits option to apply credit balances to amounts owed. 2. Review the payment entry check-box options for considering non-printed and non-finalized bills. Typically consideration for amounts not yet printed on a bill is not checked. It's common to check the consider non-finalized amounts. This issue has nothing to do with the version of PowerManager she's using. It's about how the enter payments options are checked and when she enters payments compared to when she prints bills and finalizes those bills.

Sreya Johny] Vicky, Sorry for the delay in getting back to you. We had to review this with our developers to confirm the solution. This is not related to your system version but rather to a payment entry setting. This happens because the ""Include Non-Finalized Charges"" checkbox is likely not selected during payment entry. When this option is unchecked, the system doesn't consider non-finalized charges as being owed, causing payments to be applied only to the highest priority service (water). Step 1: Fix Existing Credits Use the Transfer Credits option to redistribute existing credit balances: Go to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Click File > Transfer Credits Enter the Payment Date and Posting Date. The ""Type of Payment"" will automatically be set to ""Transfer"" Click the CR Transfer button and choose the customers with misapplied payments You'll be prompted: ""Do you want to manually save each credit transfer payment?"" - Answer YES if you want to review each customer before saving/cancelling the transfer - Answer NO to have the program automatically complete the processIf you select YES, then the payment entry form will be displayed for each customer that has both credits and arrears. • Click the “Save” button to post the transfer payment and continue searching for the next customer with both credit and arrears balances owed • Click the “Cancel” button to not post the transfer payment. You will then be asked if you wish to continue searching for the next customer with credits and arrears balances owed Step 2: Prevent Future Issues When entering payments through UB Processing or Cash Register: Make sure the ""Include Non-Finalized Charges"" checkbox is checked The ""Include Non-Printed Charges"" checkbox can typically remain unchecked This will ensure payments are properly distributed across all services The system will correctly apply payments to all services when the proper checkbox is selected. Please let us know if you have any questions or need further assistance.

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Rose, The account was set to 'Seasonal no bill' on 1/8. You guys ran your calculation update for January on 2/5. You then set it to Normal billing on 2/18. So it missed January. You would have seen this account on your Bill Code report you ran for January in your task menu: I have since added a 'Current Consumption' column to try to help give you more data for no bill accounts. To fix it, you will have to calculate the rate * consumption and just add that amount as an adjustment. Same goes for PCE and any other charge. You wont be able to use the auto adjust on here as a bill code override is different than an incorrect meter read. The easiest way as most of my clients do if something like this happens, is to just add reads to the meters with the same amount of consumption as they were in January (try to duplicate what you want to bill them), then run the charge display. BM will do the calculations for you.. write it down, then delete the reads. Then take those amounts and any other amounts you think you should have charged them for January and create a batch, add them as entry transactions then post. You've seen me do this in training. Add read, view charge display, delete read. Hope this helps. Tyler

Alex Spelic] I have a move out customer that has a bill finalized and paid for via ACH.They stated that the meter reading is incorrect and that they have a picture of the reading the day they moved out. I need help to void the bill, re-bill and refund.

Tony Williams] Alex, To correct a bill with an incorrect meter reading for your move-out customer, you'll need to void the bill, re-bill with the correct reading, and process a refund. Here's how: Void the Bill and Re-bill: Navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the posting date (use the original bill date) Click Adjust Select the move-out customer Go to the Bills tab Locate and click on the incorrect bill (it will highlight) Click the Void button Go to the Re-Bill tab, check the Post readings and calculate charges option In the meter reading grid, enter the correct reading from the customer's photo Click Save Process the Refund: Since the customer already paid via ACH: The difference between the original bill and corrected bill will create a credit on their account To refund this amount, navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Click Adjust and select the customer Go to the Charges tab Enter the refund amount (the credit balance) as a negative value Let me know if you need any clarification on these steps.
Alex Spelic] How do I print the new bill?

Sreya Johny] Once you have voided the original bill and re-billed it with the Post Readings and Calculate Charges option checked, you can go to UB > Processing > Print Bills and print the bill for that customer only.

column2: Shea Lightsey Lightsey] To the PowerManager Support Team, I am reaching out to formally document a series of ongoing system issues that have significantly disrupted our payroll, reporting, and financial reconciliation processes. These problems are unacceptable, and we require immediate, sustained support to resolve them. 1. During payroll on 2/12, the time‑off button failed to appear. I had to exit the module entirely and re‑enter for it to display. 2. On the same day, FPPA deduction amounts did not remain saved for two employees. As a result, the deductions were not taken from their paychecks, forcing the Town to cover the missed contributions so they could be deposited into the employees' retirement accounts. 3. Transmission and effective dates reverted to the prior payroll date, even after new dates were entered. The ACH file also reverted to the incorrect dates. This created a serious compliance issue, as payroll must be completed by noon. I had to manually edit the report to correct it, which should never be required. 4. Reports continue to minimize into a small window in the lower-left corner. The trial balance showed as ""open,"" but no window appeared in Task Manager. Attempting to reopen the report repeatedly resulted in the same error. 5. Health insurance deduction amounts did not save for two employees, causing distribution reports to be incorrect. After re‑entering the amounts and rerunning the report, totals still did not update. This prevents us from using the reports to pay bills accurately. 6. These reporting issues appear to be contributing to our inability to reconcile bank accounts, putting us three months behind. These issues are severely impacting our operations. We need them addressed immediately. We also need a PowerManager representative to remote in and spend the necessary time—more than just a few minutes—to diagnose and resolve these problems thoroughly. At this point, it feels as though our concerns have been pushed aside, and that is not acceptable. Please provide a clear plan of action and timeline for resolving these issues. Shea Lightsey Town Clerk/Treasurer Town of Haxtun townofhaxtun.colorado.gov

Michael Inners] Auditor is requesting that we produce reports of receivables as of specific dates. Only reports I could find use the current date. Is this possible? If so, how do I produce this report?Auditor is only on site for a limited time.Michael Inners

Jimmy Biondo] The program was designed to work on the Express version of SQL so what we can save long term is in some cases limited. In the carse of the Accounts or Aged receivables, they are live reports that show the totals as of when you run them. Because of this, we offer a way to archive the reports when printed so they can be saved as a .PDF file for long term storage. This is called Report Archiving. Regretfullly some companies have never taken advantage of this. At the end of Every month 1) Run a Close Day process if for no other reason to make sure all the Payment, Adjustment and Deposit registers are closed and printed 2) Run a Close Financial Period. I would recommend Printing the Adjustment Summary and Audit Summary as well on this screen 3) If it is the end of your Fiscal Year, when it asks if you want to ""Reset the YTD totals"", choose Yes. If not then No This last question is the Year End Closeout. 4) as soon as you have completed the closeout, go to the Report screen and run an Aged Receivables Complete for all your books Store these together for your Accounts and Auditors"

Sreya Johny] Lewis, Steps to Transmit 1099s to IRS via PowerManager To electronically transmit your 1099s to the IRS using PowerManager, follow these steps: Go to Accounts Payable > Reports > 1099 Reports And Form In the 1099 Reports And Form screen: Select the appropriate Report Design Name Enter the Reported Year for the 1099s Select the Type of 1099 (1099-NEC, 1099-MISC, etc.) Click the E-File button and Select All to open the electronic filing form Complete the electronic filing form with: Your Transmitter Control Code (TCC) obtained from the IRS Contact information (name, phone, email) Full path and filename (location) where you want the file saved Select the File Type for the system you use (IRIS or FIRE) Click Create File to generate the electronic file Upload the generated file to the IRS Important Notes Ensure all vendor information is accurate, especially Tax ID numbers and addresses You may need to edit vendor records if you encounter errors (AP > Maintenance > Vendors) The optional ""1099 Name"" field in vendor records can be used if the 1099 name differs from the standard vendor name If you haven't registered for electronic filing yet, you'll need to obtain a Transmitter Control Code (TCC) from the IRS Please let me know if you need any clarification or have additional questions about the 1099 electronic filing process.

column1: 21060667,"Rate codes not showing as active when it is active","[2026-03-03 01:29
column2: Amanda Tate] We recently raised our rates. I made a new affective date & made it active with the new rates & made the old affective date in active. Now all our rate codes are showing as in active when they aren't & we can't attach rates to our service location.

Tony Williams] Amanda, We understand the issue with your rate codes showing as inactive. This is happening because when you made the old effective date inactive, it affected the visibility of the rate codes throughout the system. How to Fix Your Rate Codes: Navigate to Utility Billing > UB Administration > Rate Code Select the Group/Income and Rate Code name you need to fix For each rate code version: Select the rate code with the old effective date Check the Active checkbox at the top portion of the screen Click Save Then select the rate code with the new effective date Verify the Active checkbox is checked Repeat for all affected rate codes Important Note: Even though you have a future effective date for your new rates, both versions of the rate code (old and new) need to be marked as ""Active"" for them to appear in lists and be attachable to service locations. The system uses the effective date to determine which rate to apply during billing, but the Active status controls whether the rate code is visible in the system. Let us know if you need any additional assistance with this.
Amanda Tate] I was able to fix the issue with this. Amanda Tate Bethesda Water Supply Corporation Office Administrator Amanda.tate@bethesdawatersupply.com"
