SHERRI WAGONER] Can someone please call me to go through the steps of recording a check versus printing it, or recording a transaction as an ACH? I don't think that the prior clerk instructed me correctly on how to do it because I can see the transaction in the vendor invoice tab, but when I bring up a transaction list for the month that vendor is not on there.
SHERRI WAGONER] I'm sorry, I forgot to include that I will be leaving in half an hour for the rest of the day. I will return to normal hours tomorrow, March 3.

Sreya Johny] Sherri, If you only enter invoices but don't complete the payment process through AP > Processing > Pay Invoices, they will not appear in the transaction list. Print Checks - Used to pay unpaid invoices by printing checks using the selected check form. Reprint Checks - Used to print previously paid invoices on checks using the selected check form. Record Checks - Used to mark unpaid invoices as paid. Stores a check number with the invoices but does not print. ACH - Used to pay unpaid invoices by creating an ACH file that is used for automated electronic payments. Here are the complete steps for Record Checks versus ACH processes: Recording a Check (without printing) Go to AP > Processing > Pay Invoices In the Payment Method Selection screen: Select Record Checks as the Payment Method Enter the Payment Date Click Next In the Bank Account Selection screen: Select the appropriate Bank Account Enter the Starting Check Number Select the vendors to be paid and click Next In the Payment Verification screen: Verify the invoices to be paid (ensure the ""OK To Pay"" box is checked) Click Finish to complete the process Recording an ACH Transaction Select ACH as the Payment Method Enter the Starting ACH Number Enter the Transmission Date and Effective Settlement Date Specify the ACH File Name Click Finish to complete the process and create the ACH file Please let me know if you need any clarification or have additional questions about these processes.
SHERRI WAGONER] This is excellent information and fills a lot of gaps that I had from previous trainings.

KRISTIN CHILDERS] I have a council meeting tonight and cannot reconcile an account. I think it is because it is I voided a check and it didn't zero out the voided check.
Susan Thomas] I will reach out to her in the morning.

Susan Thomas] Hi Kristin, Please proceed according to the instructions provided. Proposed Solution This appears to be an issue with the void process not completing correctly. The solution is to verify the void process was done properly and potentially re-do the void process to ensure the check is properly zeroed out in the system. Steps to complete 1. Navigate to the Accounts Payable module and select ""Void Checks/Unpaid Invoices"" option. 2. In the Void Selection screen: - Select ""Checks"" in the Void Selection field - Enter the appropriate GL Posting Date for when you want the void to be recorded - Select the vendor associated with the check using the ""Select Vendors"" button 3. Click ""Next"" to proceed to the Void Verification screen. 4. In the Void Verification screen: - Locate the check that needs to be voided in the list - Place a checkmark in the ""Void"" checkbox next to the check - Verify that the check is selected correctly 5. Click the ""Void"" button to complete the process and create the reversing entries. 6. Verify in the General Ledger that the voiding transactions were properly posted by reviewing the transaction history for the account. Additional Information It's important to note that voiding transactions are posted on the date specified in the ""GL Posting Date"" field. If the original check was from a different month than when it was voided, this could cause reconciliation issues. For example, if a check was issued in one month and voided in another, you may need to consider this timing difference when reconciling your accounts.

Amy Wegener] I have a new garnishment for payroll. I went in and set up the vendor and then in the garnishment, I edited it and added the vendor, but it didn't process with my payroll.

Susan Thomas] Hi Amy, Please try the following and let us know how it goes. Proposed Solution This issue is likely caused by one of two common problems with garnishment processing: 1) The garnishment needs to be properly set up with an AP vendor assigned to it, and 2) The liability GL accounts for the garnishment deduction need to be set up in Payroll Maintenance before finalizing the pay period. The garnishment is actually processed during the payroll finalization process, not during the initial payroll calculation. Steps to complete Verify that the garnishment deduction has an AP vendor properly assigned to it in the employee's deduction setup Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Setup and select the Liability Lookup tab Ensure that GL accounts have been assigned to the new garnishment deduction If the pay period has already been calculated but not finalized, go to Payroll > Processing > Update Click the Distribution button to refigure the distribution with the updated GL accounts Click the Finalize button to complete the payroll process and generate the AP invoice for the garnishment The garnishment will now appear as a payable in Accounts Payable that can be processed and paid

Sreya Johny] Need to review with Mitch if this is correct: Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets and documentation, the most efficient approach would be to add this new solar meter customer charge as a component to the existing solar rate structure rather than creating an entirely new rate. This approach is consistent with how similar charges have been handled in other municipalities using PowerManager. Steps to complete Access PowerManager and navigate to UB > Rates edits Locate and select the existing solar meter/net metering rate Click ""Add a component"" to create a new flat charge component Enter the component description (e.g., ""Solar Meter Customer Charge"") Set the amount to $5.00 Ensure ""Seasonal"" is checked if this charge applies year-round Assign the appropriate GL account number for revenue tracking Save the new component Verify the component appears correctly in the rate structure Test the rate calculation on a sample customer account to ensure it bills properly Additional Considerations If you need to track this new charge separately in reports, you may also want to update your Billing Statistics report to include this new component in an appropriate column. This can be done through UB > Reports > Financial > Billing Statistics > Setup. The system shouldn't allow it to be blank, but for some reason we are seeing several that have null values in the field, which means that they never had a model associated with them, or they did, and it got deleted somehow. Your data may have more than we have in our data, since our data is a few months old. We can run the same query on your database to find the backflows that need a model associated to them, and then associate the first model in the list. Once you have that list and can see the devices, you can determine which model should actually be associated with them. Tony Williams] To cancel one of your doubled ACH payments, you'll need to use the Void Payment function in PowerManager. Here's how: Navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the original payment date as the Posting Date Click Adjust Select the customer with the doubled payment Go to the Payments tab Locate and click on the duplicate payment in the grid (it will highlight) Click the Void button at the bottom right (only click once) Click Save once the selected payment appears in the Adjustments section This will properly reverse the duplicate payment and update the customer's account balance

Tony Williams] Marilyn, To cancel one of your doubled ACH payments, you'll need to use the Void Payment function in PowerManager. Unfortunately, you can only void one customer at a time. column2: Tonya Parson] When trying to effective date a transfer to GL, changed the day but forgot to change the month. Need transfer to post in Feb as it is the end of our fiscal year. Is there a way to delete a preliminary transfer to GL and be able to do the process again with the correct date?

Tony Williams] Tonya, For your fiscal year-end GL transfer with the incorrect date, you can use the Re-export to GL function in Impresa to correct this. Here's how: Go to UB Administration Select Re-export to GL Select the file that needs to be corrected You can then re-export with the correct February date When re-exporting, make sure to verify the date is set correctly for February since this is critical for your fiscal year-end processing. If the transfer has already been posted in your GL system, you may need to reverse that entry first before re-exporting. Let me know if you've already completed the posting in GL and I can provide additional guidance.

Nicole Frahm] Good Morning,, We are needing tot take a look at what percentage is being taken out for employees federal . We had a employee not have enough taken out for federal and now she has to pay in.

Sreya Johny] Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee > Edit and select the employee with the withholding issue Navigate to the Deductions tab to review the current federal withholding setup Verify that the correct federal withholding deduction type is assigned based on the employee's W-4 form (e.g., Single, Married, Married Step 2, etc.) If needed, you can adjust the withholding amount by: Adding an ""Extra"" amount to increase the federal withholding Setting a ""Minimum"" amount to ensure at least that amount is withheld For precise control, you can uncheck the ""Compute"" box and enter a specific amount in the ""Total"" column For the employee who now owes taxes, consider adjusting their withholding for future pay periods to prevent the same issue from occurring again Calculate and verify the new withholding amounts before processing the next payroll Can we review Earnings/Deductions Maintenance to verify the percentage being deducted for employees’ federal withholding? However, since only one employee is affected, wouldn’t that suggest the deduction percentage is set up correctly? Additional Information The issue may be related to the employee's W-4 form. If the employee recently filled out a new W-4 (2020 or later version), you may need to ensure you have the proper deduction type set up in your system. For example, if an employee checked the ""Step 2"" box on their W-4 (for multiple jobs or spouse works), a specific deduction type needs to be assigned. If you need to make more significant adjustments, remember that you can use the Edit function in Payroll to modify minimum, maximum, or extra withholding amounts to ensure adequate taxes are being withheld. Please follow the steps to create an app password and try sending a billing email to confirm everything is working. How to Create an App Password in Google Sign in to your Google Account Go to your Google Account at myaccount.google.com Sign in with the email account you want to use for sending utility bills- or try this link Navigate to Security settings Select ""Security"" from the left navigation panel Scroll down to ""Signing in to Google"" section Enable 2-Step Verification if not already enabled This is required before you can create app passwords Follow the prompts to set up 2-Step Verification if needed Create an App Password After enabling 2-Step Verification, return to the Security page Find ""App passwords"" (under ""Signing in to Google"") Select ""App passwords"" At the bottom, choose ""Select app"" and pick ""Mail"" or ""Other"" (custom name) If choosing ""Other"", enter ""PowerManager Utility Billing"" Click ""Generate"" Google will display a 16-character password - save this password without spaces as you'll need it for setup Important Notes The app password will only be shown once No spaces are needed when entering this password in PowerManager This is a secure way to allow PowerManager to send emails without using your main Google password Setting Up Email Bills in PowerManager Utility Billing Configure Email Settings in PowerManager Open PowerManager and navigate to Utility Billing Go to Maintenance > Setup > Email Setup Enter the following information: SMTP Server: smtp.gmail.com Port: 587 Use SSL/TLS: Yes (checked) Email Address: Your full Gmail address Password: The 16-character App Password you generated (without spaces) Display Name: Your utility company name Set Up Customer Email Addresses Go to Maintenance > Customer Accounts For each customer who should receive emailed bills: Open their account Navigate to the Billing tab Enter their email address Check the ""Email Bills"" option Test the Email Configuration Send a test email to yourself to confirm everything works

Tony Williams] Regina, Below is a proposed solution to the issue you mentioned. If this doesn't address your issue, please reply back and someone will reach out to you as soon as possible. Note that we are running with shorter staff due to the Holidays through the rest of this week. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation, you can use the Accounts Receivable (AR) Payments Report functionality to generate reports for specific date ranges. This will allow you to view all charges and payments within your custom date ranges regardless of how their period summary reports are processed. Steps to complete Open ISB and navigate to the Accounts Receivable module Select ""Reports"" from the menu, then choose ""Payments"" In the AR - Payments Form, make sure the ""Date Range"" tab is selected (not ""Batch Range"") For the Report Design, select an appropriate payment report format from the dropdown list (such as ""Payment Report by Customer"") Enter the specific date range needed: For the first report: Start Date: 8/28/25, End Date: 9/27/25 For the second report: Start Date: 9/28/25, End Date: 10/27/25 For the third report: Start Date: 10/28/25, End Date: 11/27/25 In the ""Sort By"" field, select the preferred sorting method (customer name, customer ID, etc.) Click ""Preview"" to view the report on screen, or ""Print"" to send it directly to the printer Repeat the process for each date range needed Additional Information If you need to see both charges and payments together in a more comprehensive format, you might also consider using the ""Items"" report under the AR Reports menu. This report allows for date range selection and can display invoice/charge information along with payment data, depending on the report format selected.

Sreya Johny] Kristina, If you prefer not to create a new invoice, you can reprint the check under Pay Invoices using the Reprint Checks option. Enter the original Payment Date and select the vendor account. Enter the original check number in the Starting Check Number to Reprint, Ending Check Number to Reprint, and First New Check Number fields, since you’ll be keeping the same check number. Click Next. On the next screen, you may receive a warning about the same check number being used. This can be safely ignored, simply close the warning and proceed to Print. Shea Lightsey Lightsey] I am processing payroll, I entered in the deductions for two employees that are supposed to come out of their checks. I clicked save, checked it and they were in there. I started the payroll process and went to check payroll history and the deductions aren't coming out. I went into the employees file and the deductions that I just entered in were gone. This is a huge issue and needs fixed before noon. Shea Lightsey Town Clerk/Treasurer Town of Haxtun townofhaxtun.colorado.gov

Susan Thomas] Proposed Solution This issue is likely caused by not recalculating payroll after making changes to employee deductions. Any changes made to employee earnings, deductions, or employer amounts require recalculation through the Payroll Processing menu before they will be properly applied to the current payroll cycle. Steps to complete Re-enter the deductions for the two employees and save them Go to Payroll > Processing > Calculate Enter the pay period ending date in the ""Pay Period Ending"" field Enter the check printed date in the ""Check Date"" field Click the ""Calculate"" button and select the affected employees Verify the deductions now appear correctly in the payroll history If needed, use the ""Edit"" button in the Calculate form to manually verify or adjust deduction amounts for individual employees Additional Troubleshooting (if above solution doesn't work) Check Payroll > Processing > Calculate > Setup to ensure the ""Use this list to compute deductions"" checkbox is properly configured and that the relevant deductions are checked to be included in calculations Verify in Payroll > Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions that the deductions have appropriate frequency settings (check the ""Monthly Pay Period To Be Used"" values - a value of zero indicates it should always be used) Check for any ""False"" rows in Void Paychecks that might be interfering with the current payroll cycle (navigate to PR > Void Paychecks and look for any ""False"" entries that need to be removed)

Jackie Jackson] Good Morning, We identified several accounts where penalties were applied even though the balance was zero at the time.In the screenshot, customer account #060003923 was charged $77.50. The due date was 12/10/25, and the payment was applied on 12/12/25 at 3:21 p.m. via a batch. Penalties were run at 3:28 p.m., 3% interest was applied despite a zero balance.The other screenshot shows the same issue occurring on account #060004976. We also ran a report and identified the following accounts that were affected on that day, including the two mentioned above. 060003923, 060004976, 060003276, 060003462Thank you, Jackie
Chris Holloway] Hi Jackie, I will jump on your server to take a look at this in the system, then we can determine what our next steps will be.

Chris Holloway] Hi Jackie, We were not able to determine definitively what occurred, however, the penalties were applied within 5 minutes of the payments being processed. If the Apply Penalties screen was already open and the cycle had been previewed before the payments were processed, then the system would have applied the penalties to the accounts on the screen and not checked again before the apply was done. Another scenario that I just thought about is if the preview was done after you applied the pending payments, but before the server finished processing them to the accounts. If there were a large number of payments in view pending, the apply penalties process could paused the apply payments process to populate the preview list, which means any payments not processed up to that point, would have been included in the penalty preview. The payments would have continued processing after that. To keep this from happening in the future, be sure to post your payments before the apply penalties screen is opened, and it you want to be more certain, wait about 15 minutes before previewing the penalties. This will ensure that the payments have completed processing from the batch and the server has updated all the records. If you wait the recommended 15 minutes next time, and you still see this issue happening, put in another ticket as a Severity 1. We will need a backup of your data pulled to our side so that we can reproduce the problem for our developers."

Christie Nutt] Good morning I hit verify/reapply instead of verifying while processing billing. I needed to verify and then apply charges. Did I mess anything up? ​​ Christie Nutt Controller D: (512) 304-0368

Tony Williams] Christie, The verify/reapply function is different from just verifying charges. When you use verify/reapply, the system shows what would happen if you were to remove the previous charges and apply new ones based on the current readings. The good news is that using verify/reapply by itself doesn't make any actual changes to your accounts. It only shows what would happen if you were to complete the reapply process. No changes are made until you actually click the ""Reapply"" button after reviewing the verification report. To confirm everything is still correct: Check your ledger entries for the affected accounts to make sure no unexpected charges or credits were applied If you haven't clicked the actual ""Reapply"" button after the verify/reapply, then your original charges should still be intact You can now proceed with your normal verification of Verify/Apply and apply process as intended
Christie Nutt] Good News!! ​​ Christie Nutt Controller D: (512) 304-0368

Diana Quintana] I would like to know why the unbilled balance amount is showing in the Customer payment screen. It is confusing since the invoices will be sent out until next Monday.

Tony Williams] Diana, The unbilled balance amount is showing in the Customer payment screen because charges have already been entered into the system, but haven't been officially billed yet since invoices won't be sent until next Monday. This is normal system behavior in Impresa. The system tracks these charges as ""unbilled balances"" until the invoices are generated and sent out. Once the invoices are sent on Monday, these amounts will move from unbilled to billed status. The unbilled balance display allows staff to see what charges are pending for customers even before the official billing occurs. This can be helpful when customers call with questions about their accounts. Let me know if you have any other questions about how the unbilled balances work in the system.

Diana Quintana] Good morning, I understand the unbilled balance will appear in the Customer tab under Account Maintenance. However, it should not appear under the Customer Payment Tab until the invoices are sent. This creates confusion when taking payments, and it is not how it normally shows in the system. I've attached receipts showing the difference between last month and the current one. Diana Quintana

Hi Alysia, Licensing doesn't take into account actual users, just the number of total users that can connect at any given time. As long as there are licensed spots available, you can use the software. This is different than User Logins. A user login (for Cloud connections) do require a username/password. The user name and password can be shared between users, but only one login can access the application at any given time. The license file allows for up to 5 logins accessing the software at a single time. I hope this helps.

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Alysia, Yes, they are two different things. License File: Allows a max number of users to use Billmaster at any given time. This is the charge to use the software and doesn't matter if on premise or in the cloud. Just max spots. User Login: This only applies to Cloud Based Billmaster setups. Since PDS is on the Cloud, we get billed by Microsoft to use Azure cloud hosting services, so we then charge the client for each login. You can have as many logins as you want, however you have to pay for them and only a certain number can be connected to Billmaster at any given time due to the license file limitations. Tyler Silvernail] solution 1 Hi Christi, This isn't a duplicate account. You have the same name record listed twice on the account, one as the Tenant and one as the Landlord. You can disable one name record and set it to active = False if you want. You can do that from the home screen. T

Tyler Silvernail] Solution 2 Hi Christi, Not exactly sure what the complaint is. Once the name record is associated with the account, they can click through the accounts and view/print their statements. Once you guys start sending emails after billing they will get them emailed to them and that might be easier to print? Or they can set their ebill settings back to print and just have you mail them the statements as it defeats the purpose of ebill emails. I understand that it's a process to click/view/print but they manage multiple properties with multiple statements. Another long term solution is to set up summary billing in Billmaster, were you have a parent account and child accounts associated with it. It's do-able but will require a bit more work for you and Rose each month as you have to take payments and disperse them to each of the accounts for the customer and run processing to create a single bill. You also have to set the accounts up and maintain them once summary billing is enabled. Right now the customer is doing the work, if you want to do it for them we can discuss it further. T

Tyler Silvernail] Solution 3 Hi Rose, How do you want the money to be handled on the new account? Is it going to be used for a new deposit or just a credit to be applied to charges? Either way, create a batch and use the transfer Credit OUT transaction code, then use the transfer credit IN on the new account for the same amount. If it is to be used for a deposit, just use your normal Deposit Charge Transaction codes and when the credit transfers IN it will be applied to the deposit charges. T

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Christi, It's up to you guys. If both accounts qualify for the PCE with two separate name records you can set it up that way. If you only want the one name record associated with the accounts, then you'll need to remove the PCE charge from one of the accounts. If they are separate name record IDs, then they will only see the accounts with that name record ID. Do Tana and Adam both qualify for PCE? If so then they need two separate Member numbers. Tricia Kuszak] I don""t think the ACH payments posted to the customers accounts. When I ran bills it appears that it did not. Is there possibly a step I forgot?

Mitch Dahl] You could spot check a few bank pay customers in Customer Lookup on the History tab and see if a payment shows up or not. If not, then do the ACH Pay from UB-Processing-Enter Payments. After that, re-compute the bank pay customers in Compute Charges with the OK to calculate printed bills checkbox checked, but only for the ACH customers. Finally print the bills for those customers again.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Tricia, We recommend spot checking a few customers to see if their payment shows on their account. You can look via Customer Lookup on the History tab for a payment. If you don't see those payments, you might've missed the second step. Remember when you process ACH payments created with PowerManager's ACH File, you must post the payments on the account after sending the file to the bank. You post those payments by going to UB --> Processing --> Enter Payments, click the ACH Pay button, select the ACH file, and click Ok. After that, re-compute the bank pay customers in Compute Charges with the OK to calculate printed bills checkbox checked, but only for the ACH customers. Finally print the bills for those customers again.
Tricia Kuszak] Tricia Kuszak Deputy Clerk City of Loup City"

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Can you also please send a screenshot of that customer's account history from Customer Lookup and their account balance?
Michelle Roach] Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Mitch Dahl] This is usually associated with the rate setup in the Add To # column. The usage component is probably adding to something for which there is no charge. Typically the Add To # for the usage component is blank and other components add to it.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Michelle. Changed the Add To setup per Mitch. Previewed a bill and it's corrected now. Also confirmed the programming change we did to their bill for the late fee is correct.
Michelle Roach] Some customers have the 2/20/26 bill on the right side and some do not. Why is this? Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Hi Michelle, Did you know if Bev Hansen had a bill printed at all?
Michelle Roach] Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] I'm glad to see it did print a bill for her. Are there many accounts that are like this? It'd be good to double check the bill printing setup. If you go to UB --> Processing --> Print Bills --> Setup. Under the PDF Archive Files we should see both options checked (create pdf archive files AND create pdf archive files when reprinting). In the PDF archive file template, we should see Use the following template and your bill selected in the drop down field.

Michelle Roach] Yes there are quite a few like this. Also we didn’t talk about the dates on the bills. They read 1/20-2/20. Do I manually change this and where? Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] If the pdf archive checkboxes weren't checked in setup, it's possible that's why some customers don't have the record. I think as long as you can still access the bill information, it is ok. Make sure to double check those bill printing settings and if it continues to happen, we can try to see what else might be causing the issue. As for the dates, those are correct. If I remember correctly, your last charges date or bill printing date in January was 1/20. So the bill is recording the correct service from service to dates based on the date those previous transactions were generated.

Michelle Roach] No those dates are not correct. They should be from when we read which is usually the first week of the month to the next month around the same time. It cannot be the 20th. That is the date I do bills and mail them out. The service is prior to that. This has to change. Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] If you can do me a favor and open the Meter Readings history for someone's account and send a screenshot? You can access this by going to UB --> Customer Lookup --> Select your customer --> Meter --> Readings.
Michelle Roach] Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Mitch Dahl] Typically, the service from and service to dates come from the meter reading dates. Might have to look at the bill design to see if there is an issue there.

Michelle Roach] Emelia I need you to call me today. On Paystar we noticed the new bills are not on there. When I told you the bills were not showing up on the right side of each customers account on the account lookup page, that was a problem. Please call Michelle Roach Deputy Clerk City of Tekamah (402)374-2521
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Called, Michelle stepped out to run errands. I will call back in ~20 minutes.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Michelle. There was not Monthly Bill File created with Feb's billing, so the statements weren't updated on Paystar's portal. Walked Michelle through the steps again to create the export file to be manually uploaded. She asked how to upload it to Paystar, I advised she'd have to contact Paystar for that information. She also said the balances were incorrect in the Paystar portal. I confirmed the daily balance upload has been processing daily at 8:15am CST and uploading successfully to Paystar's FTP site. We also confirmed the balances in that file are correct. It might be an issue on Paystar's end as to why the balances aren't updating correctly. Sent a follow up email to Abel and CC'd Michelle. We also looked at the dates of service on the bill. For every bill, the dates were what they should be: the prior and present reading dates from the meter. I don't know where Michelle saw the date of 1/20, but that wasn't on any of the Jan or Feb bill statements. Confirmed with Michelle they want to see the meter reading dates there, so no corrections were needed as it was functioning as intended.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Pasting from Outlook, sent to Mitch M: Hey Mitch, I have a bit of a conundrum with Tekamah’s most recent billing. I’ll do my Michelle printed bills and the majority of people don’t have a pdf record in their Bill tab in Customer History. Some do, so I don’t think it’s a setup issue in Bill Printing. She originally said the service from/to dates were wrong. One bill she sent as an example in the ticket showed dates of 1/20-2/20. When I checked the bill design today, the dates were correct from the meter reading record. Not sure how she produced a bill with 1 set of dates and then I see something different today. When producing the paystar daily balance file (with the “All” option selected for which charges to include), the balances for most customers were 0.00, even though their account had a balance. I change the charges selection to “Finalized”, produced a new file, and the balances were correct. I have a feeling this has something to do with the strange bill pdfs & date issues. Do you know what could’ve happened to cause these issues? I find it all very suspect. I wonder if Michelle did something or missed something? More importantly, do these sound like 1-off issues to you or a potential recurring problem?

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Good morning Michelle, I wanted to update you on what might be the issue with this month's bills. It's possible this happened if the bills were printed from the preview screen. What I'd recommend to fix it is to print bills (not reprint, just regular print) to a pdf file on your computer to record all bills as printed and to also create the pdf bills in Customer Lookup history.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Michelle. We printed bills for all and saved to a PDF. There were about 1/2 the customers in this group. Confirmed the accounts that were missing the history before now have the PDF."

Sreya Johny] Dori, You can use the built-in Time Off report in PowerManager's Payroll module to create a time off request sheet showing employee balances for vacation, sick, and comp time. Navigate to Payroll > Reports > Employee > Time Off In the PR - Time Off Form that appears, you'll see these options: Select a report format from the Report Grid Choose to run for current pay period or specify a date range Option to include department subtotals Option to include inactive employees Sort employees by various criteria Click the Preview button to view the report, which will show Start, Earned, Used, and Balance values for each time off type Please let us know if you have any questions.

Tricia Kuszak] I have An emplyee whose ACH won't print off. I want to make sure our machine operator gets paid.

Susan Thomas] Proposed Solution This appears to be an issue with the ACH payment process in the Payroll module. The problem could be related to missing bank account information for the employee, incorrect setup in the Print Checks ACH process, or an issue with the ACH file generation. Steps to complete Verify that the employee has proper bank account information entered in their employee record. Missing bank account information is a common cause of ACH printing issues. Navigate to Payroll/Processing menu and select Print Checks. In the Payment Method section, select ACH (for direct deposit). Enter the Check Date, Pay Period Start Date, and Pay Period End Date. Click Next or Select Employees to proceed with employee selection. After selecting employees, proceed to the ACH setup form (Step 3) where you'll need to enter: Transmission date Effective settlement date ACH file name Click Next to proceed to the final step where you'll verify the employee list. Make sure there's a check mark in the ""OK To Print"" box for the employee in question. Click Print to generate the direct deposit forms and create the ACH file. Confirm that the forms printed correctly when prompted. Additional Notes If the issue persists, it may be necessary to recalculate the payroll amounts before attempting to print the ACH forms again. Based on similar tickets, sometimes adding or verifying bank accounts for employees and then recalculating can resolve the issue.

Leslee Merritt] Our Oasis database is too large. In the event of an emergency, we would not be able to restore the Oasis test from Production.

Tony Williams] Leslee, I understand your concern about the Oasis database size. We have some other customers with very large databases. This is typically caused by large log files that don't automatically shrink after backups. The log file (oasis.ldf) can grow significantly over time, especially if: Your database is in ""Full"" recovery mode Large reports or queries are run regularly Backups aren't clearing logs properly To resolve this: Check if your database is in ""Simple"" recovery model Use SQL Management Studio to shrink the log file: Right-click the database Select Tasks > Shrink > Files Choose ""Log"" as the file type Shrink the file This should free up enough space to allow test database restoration in emergency situations.

BRANDY BOLTER] We are looking at adding a senior citizen discount to our utility bills. I am needing someone to contact me or send me instructions on how we would go about adding this to the bills.

Susan Thomas] Option 1: Create a Discount Component in Rate Maintenance Navigate to Utility Billing → Maintenance → Rate → Components Create a new component specifically for senior citizen discounts Set the component type as ""Discount"" Enter an appropriate description (e.g., ""Senior Citizen Discount"") Set the calculation method (typically percentage-based) Enter the discount percentage amount Ensure the ""Active"" checkbox is selected Navigate to Utility Billing → Maintenance → Rate → Rates Edit each rate where you want to apply the senior discount Add the senior discount component to the rate Set the ""Season"" to ""ALL"" to apply year-round Option 2: Apply Discounts Through Adjustments For individual accounts that qualify for the senior discount: Navigate to Utility Billing → Processing → Adjustments Select the customer account On the ""Charges"" tab, enter the adjustment amount for the discount If the discount affects taxable charges, use the ""Calc Tax"" button to properly calculate tax adjustments Save the adjustment Identifying Senior Citizens in the System To track which customers qualify for the senior discount: Navigate to Utility Billing → Maintenance → Customer → Customer Edit the customer account You can use one of these methods to identify senior citizens: Add a note in the customer's account Use a custom field if available Create a specific customer type for senior citizens

Tony Williams] Good morning Patty, I've looked into the issue you're experiencing with applying non-metered accounts. This appears to be related to a custom setting in the Impresa database that's preventing the apply function from working properly, even though verify apply shows the correct information. We've seen this exact issue before with other customers. There's a database switch called ""ignore_save_nm"" that might be turned on in your system. When this switch is active, it can cause the ""sorry no data for this report"" error during the apply process. To resolve this, we'll need to run a simple database script to turn this switch off. I can help coordinate this with you. Before we proceed, could you confirm if you've closed your reading books first? This is sometimes required before applying non-metered accounts. Let me know when would be a good time to connect and address this issue, as I understand you need to apply these accounts today.
Chris Holloway] Hi Patty, I called a few minutes ago and will schedule to give you a call back Monday around 9am your time to help with this.

Patty McGee] Chris, Never mind. I needed help on Feb. 24th. We reset or restarted the server, and I closed the books again. We don’t know why it didn’t work the first time. I did find out there was a report to see it didn’t work. Out of curiosity what would happen if I tried to close the books twice. Patty

Chris Holloway] Hi Patty, If any account's showed up in the list when you close the reading books as an error, then the books would not close for any of the accounts at all. If you close the reading books, and it is successful, then close them again, all the accounts would show up as errors. I have attached a short video that shows how to print a report to show which books have been closed in impresa. Download and unzip the .zip file to view the video.

Tana Valle] We have a final bill on an account that had the reading that was entered incorrectly. It shows 9112000 usage from pump station 7. Is there a way we can go in and get this changed?

Tony Williams] Yes, you can correct the final reading. You'll need to use the Re-read feature in the Final Bill process. As long as no new readings have been applied to the meter at the service location that was attached to the final bill account. Also, you cannot have already close the reading books again for the book the meter is in. Attach the Service Location that was previously attached. Go to the customer's account Click the Final Bill button on the Process menu Proceed through the wizard to the screen where you enter readings Check the Re-read box Enter the correct reading This will recalculate the charges based on the new reading. Note that this will only work if you haven't closed the reading books or done an apply since the final bill was generated. If those actions have already been done, you'll need to adjust the account manually.

AMBER WYNN] Please see attached document for reference.When printing reminders, the final account has been listed twice. This has happened more than once. If I delete one of the two duplicates, both are deleted. Please review and advise.

Susan Thomas] Hello Amber, Proposed Solution The duplicate entry problem may be related to how accounts are being selected or filtered when generating reminder notices. Similar issues have been resolved by adjusting date parameters or checking specific account selection criteria. Steps to Complete Access the Reminders feature through Processing
column3: Reminders in the Utility Billing menu Review the ""Type of Included Accounts"" selection in the Reminders-Setup form to ensure appropriate filtering Check the date parameters (particularly the Ending Date) to confirm they are set correctly Verify whether the ""Ignore ending date for payments"" checkbox is appropriately set for your needs Run a test print with these adjusted settings to see if the duplicate entry issue is resolved If the issue persists, consider selecting specific customers manually rather than using automated filters Additional Information The duplicate entries issue appears to be related to how the system processes the final account in the reminder notice batch. This could be a software bug that needs to be addressed in a future update. In the meantime, the steps above should provide a workable solution.

Stephanie Metzger] I accidentally overbilled an account due to an incorrect electrical read being input into Power Manager. How do I fix this?

Tony Williams] Team, This doesn't look right to me, but I may be missing something. - Tony ------------------ Stephanie, To fix the overbilling due to an incorrect electrical reading: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the original Posting Date and click Adjust Select the customer account Go to the Bills tab and select the bill that needs correction Click the Void button Go to the Re-Bill tab Enter the correct meter reading Check the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" box Save the changes Print and finalize the new bill Make sure to complete all steps, especially printing and finalizing the new bill, even if the customer doesn't need a physical copy.
Sreya Johny] Looks good to me. I’ll make it public.

Joe Sheehan] Issue The customer from City of Kimball entered an incorrect batch number (4134) when posting a batch on 2/18/26. The correct batch number should have been 4136. The receipts and other information are correct, but the posted batch number is incorrect and needs to be updated. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, there are two possible approaches to resolve this issue. Since the batch has already been posted, we cannot simply reopen it as we might with a closed but unposted batch. Instead, we will need to use the Table Utilities program to update the batch number in the relevant database tables. Steps to complete Connect remotely with the customer to verify the details of the incorrect batch posting. Run the Transaction Item Report and UB-Payment Detail Report to confirm which transactions need to be corrected and verify the current batch number. Use the Table Utilities program to identify all payments associated with batch #4134 that should be updated to batch #4136. Execute an update query to change the batch number from 4134 to 4136 in the appropriate tables (likely including the CRTransPay table and possibly UB payment tables). Run reports after the update to verify that the batch number has been correctly changed to 4136. Confirm with the customer that the reports now show the correct batch number and that everything balances properly. Key people that may be able to assist Mitch Dahl - Has extensive experience with batch corrections and has helped with similar issues in multiple tickets. Susan Thomas - Has assisted with batch number corrections and worked with Mitch on similar issues. Additional Notes This issue appears to be purely a data correction and should not affect the actual payment processing or customer accounts, as the customer mentioned that ""Receipts etc. are okay."" The correction is needed to ensure accurate record-keeping and reporting. Based on similar tickets, this type of correction is routinely handled using the Table Utilities program to update the relevant database tables. Referenced Documents 106636: Deposit off- payment entered incorrectly 105539: batch number for UB deposit 103282: Batch off by one penny 105774: correct a deposit batch 92523: Wrong Payment Amount In CR

Sreya Johny] Good morning Holly, You can void the duplicate payment in Utility Billing through Adjustments and you just have to make not of it on your Cash Register report(s). To void a payment: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments > Enter the correct Posting date (use the date of the original payment) > Adjust > Select the customer > Go to Payments tab > Select the duplicate payment to be voided and then click the Void button only once at the bottom right > Click Save once the selected payment appears in the Adjustments section at the bottom. Sachin Yadav] There was a step missing while setting up the GL code. We helped Heather to walk through all the steps while setting up the GL code. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sachin

Chris Holloway] Hi Heather, We were able to set up a COA Distribution Code for this. This is found in System Administration > GL Configuration > Distribution Codes. We put in the 1013 account in the search field, then set the Alternate COA string to 1015. doing this send 1015 in place of any 1013 on the export to GL report. There is also a flag on the Distribution Codes screen that will display this changed GL on the reports as well. The alternative would be to go to each screen that 1013 is set up as a distribution or COA/GL and change each of those over to the 1015 one by one. This includes any rate codes, adjustment codes, direct GL codes, groups and income centers, Penalty codes, the default suspense account on the payment information screen, and any other screen that has distribution codes like in the inventory if you use it. I may be forgetting a few places as well. We can also probably create a script that will replace these all at one time, as a custom script. This would have to be sent to our PS and Sales Department for review."

Susan Thomas] Issue Michelle McBride from the City of Fairbury needs help modifying a report called ""Outifi Report"". She wants to remove the city/state/zip columns because they are pulling information from the mailing address instead of the location. She would like to replace these columns with Service Address 2 from the location section of the accounts. The report already correctly pulls Service Address 1 into the Location Address column, which she wants to keep unchanged. Proposed Solution Based on similar tickets, this issue can be resolved by modifying the report using the PowerManager Report Designer. We need to remove the city/state/zip columns that currently pull data from the mailing address and add a field for Service Address 2 from the location section instead. This will ensure the report shows the correct location information rather than mailing information. Steps to complete Connect remotely with Michelle to access her PowerManager system Open the ""Outifi Report"" in the Report Designer Remove the existing city/state/zip columns that are pulling from the mailing address Add a new column for Service Address 2 from the location data Ensure the Location Address column (Service Address 1) remains unchanged Save the modified report and run a test to verify the changes meet Michelle's requirements Confirm with Michelle that the report now displays the correct information

column1: 21040949,"Power Manager not sending updated account balances back to online bill pay","[2026-02-20 03:42
column2: LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Hello, Not sure if this is something you can help me with. I have also contacted online payment company, so waiting for a reply back from them also. It has come to our attention that the Power Manager program is not sending back the updated account balances back to our online payment system for the customers to see their current balances. We are able to import the payments from the online bill pay but they are not receiving the updated account balances.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Pasting from Outlook: Hello Emelia, I was reaching out to you because we updated our computers a month ago and I noticed last week that Power Manager isn't sending the file back every morning to update the account balances. Is this something that you can help me with or can you let me know who can? I put in a ticket with Power Manger last week but haven't heard anything on that yet. Lacy McLaughlin Pawnee County RWD #1

Joe Sheehan] Reached out to Lacy. The UBDailyfileExport application had a my in front of the name so we removed that and launched the application added a path to it and verified that it uploaded just fine. Lacy is going to check on Nebraska Payport to make sure it uploaded the balances.

LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe, I waited until today to check on those balances and it is still not uploading the balances. The account i checked said they owed 122.27 and they actually have a credit of 63.77. If we can look at that again that would be great.
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, I tried calling but it just rang and rang with no answering machine. Feel free to give me a call back at your convenience. My number is 401-900-5617. Joe
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, Correction my number is 401-900-5671 sorry about that.

Joe Sheehan] Lacy called me back. We're thinking she had site specific changes on her old machine that are not on her new machine. She's going to see if she can get her old machine back up and running and we'll see about transferring those site-specific changes.
Joe Sheehan] Found the site-specific change exe file. Still need to get onto the old computer and find out what the settings were.

Joe Sheehan] We were able to get the exe file in place and ran without error attaching updated one here. Uploaded the file to Payport via FTP and waiting on a response from an email Lacy sent out. : We sent a file via FTP and we noticed that the balances are not changing online. Attached is a screen shot of the FTP window and the daily balances file.
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, Did you ever hear anything back from Payport support? Joe

LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe this is what i received from them yesterday afternoon.Hey I might be finding an issue. Have you changed how your files are uploaded to our server lately? It appears that the balance files started showing up to us in a different format after Feb 23rd. I can put you in contact with the power manager people that handle most of the generation of your balance files and sending them over to our server. I apologize for the inconvenience you are facing and just want to be sure I load the data in properly. It looks like you had different file formats on Feb 23rd (the original file format you were using), feb 24th (a new format I had not seen before), and feb 25 (a second new format different from the one on the 24th). I just believe something changed in how your files are generated and sent to us somewhere within that time. If you need to contact the power manager people who handle this sort of issue, my contact for them is Emelia De Smet-Bacon and she can be reached at EDeSmet-Bacon@harriscomputer.com. If you have any other questions or concerns please let me know. SamTyler Nebraska Support

Joe Sheehan] @Emelia De Smet-Bacon, this is a strange one. When I went into the batch file, I noticed that the batch file was trying to run a program with ""My"" prepended to it. But when I looked in TeamSupport it wasn't showing they have any site-specific changes and they didn't have the MyUBICDailyExport application on their computer. So, with Mitch D's help I located the site-specific install file on the server. After I installed the site-specific changes, I keep getting this error when trying to launch the MyUBICDailyExport.exe file. We found the files in this location on the server: So, what I did was found another company with the MyUBICDailyExport.exe application and copied that from the PManAD2020 folder and that got us to the point where we could use the application and enter in the fields. Now Tyler Nebraska Support is saying the format has changed in the file export. Can we ask them to adapt or how should we go about this? Joe

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Good morning Joe, Interesting. The typical app we use for NE Payport daily balance files is called ""UBNIDailyExport"". There is a difference in the format between UBNIDailyExport and UBICDailyExport. I don't really know anything about the custom ""MyUBICDailyExport"" app. It's strange that they had MyUBIC and it was working before but now it's not... Do we know what or why we used a custom MyUBICDailyExport? If there's no reason why, I think we could change them to the standard UBNIDailyExport.

Joe Sheehan] @Emelia De Smet-Bacon, we migrated their PowerManager computers a little while ago and since we didn't have any documentation about them having site-specific changes they never got installed on their new computers. So the MyUBICDailyExport stopped working. I tried changing them back to the standard UBICDailyExport but the file format is completely different and Sam from Tyler Nebraska Support is saying the format has changed. I also tried to install the site-specific changes but they weren't working, so I grabbed a myUBICDailyExport from another customer's folder and I got that working but Sam is still saying the format is not right. Should we ask Sam to adapt or have Mitch M look at this one?
Joe Sheehan] Oh wait I see what your saying now. Use the UBNIDailyExport application right?

Joe Sheehan] Called and remoted into Lacy's computer. We updated the batch file to run the UBNIDailyExport.exe rather than the myUBICDailyExport and the file size was the same as it was before leading us to think Payport will be able to accept this file from now on. Lacy is going to check in about 15 minutes and see if the balance on one of the accounts is update and let me know.
LACY MCLAUGHLIN] I just checked the balances and they are still not correct. I think its been about 25 minutes or so.

Joe Sheehan] Lacy, Are you able to reach out to Sam for Tyler Nebraska Support and verify they got the file? Maybe he can share some more insight as to how often they scrape those directories. Or maybe we still don't have the correct format? If you can CC me on the email: Jsheehan@harriscomputer.com Joe
LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe,I will send them a message and find out more info. I feel like it should work.

LACY MCLAUGHLIN] Joe,The pull that happens at 8:15 didn't update the totals but Sam did another manual pull and it updated the totals. So i think this is fixed but confused at why it didn't update with the 8:15 pull. But looks like the online totals are correct right now.
Joe Sheehan] Lacy, That's wonderful news. I guess it just depends on when their system runs the script to pick up the file. Glad it's working now. I'll go ahead and mark this as closed. Joe"

AMANDA CARRIER] Unable to print checks. Getting following message.

Joe Sheehan] Issue Customer Amanda Carrier from City of Scribner is unable to print checks in PowerManager. The customer reports getting an error message, though the specific error message wasn't included in the initial ticket. Proposed Solution Based on reviewing similar tickets, there are several common causes for check printing issues in PowerManager. Since we don't have the specific error message, I'll recommend a series of troubleshooting steps that have resolved similar issues for other customers. Steps to complete Perform a system update and reboot the computer. This simple step resolved the issue in ticket #107587. If the error persists, try using the Data File Maintenance utility to fix any index issues: Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > GL 2 Data File Maintenance Select the correct company Choose the ""Reindex tables"" option Complete the process and try printing checks again If the system shows checks have already been printed when they haven't, try: Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > PR Data File Maintenance Go to File > Un-print non-updated payroll checks Select the appropriate employees Click OK and try printing again If you're getting a specific error related to report generation, try clicking ""Ignore All"" on any error messages and proceed. Check the design of the check form by clicking the ""Design"" button in the Print Checks process. Ensure the detail band height is not set to exactly 11 inches (if it is, change it to 10.99 inches). Key people that may be able to assist Joe Sheehan - Resolved similar check printing issues in tickets #107587 and #105648 Mitch Dahl - Resolved check printing issues in tickets #103631 and #106434 Susan Thomas - Assisted with check printing issues in ticket #92389 Mitch Mullins - Provided technical insight on check printing errors in ticket #92389 Referenced Documents 107587: PRINTING CHECKS - Cannot generate report error 103631: AP Checks Printed Out Of Order 105648: Missed Chat 106434: UNABLE TO PRINT CHECK 92389: Red Cloud - From voicemail

SELECT DISTINCT ublocation.Route, ublocation.AcctNr, ubcustomer.CustNr, scaddress.Name, ubmeter.MeterNr, ubbillinginfo.RateKey FROM ublocalcust ublocalcust INNER JOIN ubcustomer ubcustomer ON (ubcustomer.""Key"" = ublocalcust.CustomerKey) INNER JOIN scaddress scaddress ON (scaddress.""Key"" = ubcustomer.AddressKey) LEFT OUTER JOIN ubbillinginfo ubbillinginfo ON (ubbillinginfo.LocalCustKey = ublocalcust.""Key"") INNER JOIN ublocation ublocation ON (ublocation.""Key"" = ublocalcust.LocalKey) INNER JOIN ubmeter ubmeter ON (ubmeter.LocalKey = ublocalcust.LocalKey) INNER JOIN ubmeterbif ubmeterbif ON (ubmeterbif.MeterKey = ubmeter.""Key"" AND ubmeterbif.BifKey = ubbillinginfo.""Key"") WHERE ( ubbillinginfo.Status = 0 ) AND ( ubbillinginfo.RateKey = 13 ) ORDER BY ublocation.SortedRoute, ublocation.SortedAcctNr

Joe Sheehan] Solution: SELECT DISTINCT ubcustomer.CustNr, ublocation.Route, ublocation.AcctNr, scaddress.Name, ublocation.Addr1, ubbillinginfo.RateKey, ubmeter.MeterNr, ubmeter.RadioID, ubmeter.Mult, ubmeter.Form, scaddress.Phone, ublocation.LoadPoint FROM ubcustomer ubcustomer

column1: 21040348,"Utility Bills - Light meter reading and consumption not showing","[2026-02-20 00:34
column2: Sandra Schendt] Last month when we printed out the utility bills there were multiple lines for water, and lights. So we fixed the problem so it will show 1 line for each. When looking over the utility bills for this month. The water shows the meter reading and the consumption, but the light does not. The light shows the charge only. I've attached a pdf file showing you what I'm talking about. Any suggestions on how to fix this? I will reprint out the post cards if need be.
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Hi Sandra, This one I'm going to need to talk to the developers about. I've reached out to them and will let you know the answer as soon as I hear back.

Sandra Schendt] Would it be possible to go back into the utility billing rates for lights and take out the part that puts all of the rates together? Form example: base rate and consumption rate separated. I would rather have multiple listings of light rates so the meter readings and consumption show vs see the dollar amount only? That will give the programmers more time to figure it out, and I can get the utility bills in the mail?

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Yes, that's absolutely possible. To make that change, you can edit the rates. Go to UB --> Maintenance --> Rate --> Rates. Double click each rate to edit. In this section, you can delete the entry to remove the Add To #. Make sure you make the change for all rates.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Hi Sandra, Will you please provide us with a copy of your bill file? If you open File Explorer and go to your PowerManager folder, we're looking for a file called something like ""Nelson Postcard Bill.rtm"". Can you please attach a copy of that to this ticket?
Sandra Schendt] It's dated November 2025. Is there a way to make a more current file?
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Yes, there is a way to make sure it has the most recent changes. When we connect tomorrow for the other ticket, I will update & grab this file.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] The issue with the readings & usage not showing up has to do with the Add To column. We've changed that so it should show the readings now. However, Nelson has double components in their rates. Need to ask the Mitchs about Advised Sandra I would reach out early next week with an answer.

Mitch Dahl] Are you saying they have winter/summer usage components, but only a single base charge with the Season set to All? If that's the case, yes, they should have winter/summer base charge components also.
Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Meeting schedule 3/12 to go over outstanding issues: 2 customers with bills who didn't print adding a 2nd, duplicate flat rate to add to summer vs winter usage components

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Met with Sandra. Used a SQL select query to find all meters with ReadNrMax of 2. These are the meters that would use the electric components with ""R2."" We confirmed the accounts with those demand meters are all assigned the demand rate, not the other electric rates. So, we removed the R2 components from the other electric rates. We then added a summer flat rate and added that to the summer components. We edited the existing flat component to be set for winter. Advised of the 2 individuals without bills to see if it happens again next month. It could be a fluke. If it happens again next month, we will investigate. Sandra also asked about one customer's budget billing amount, we edited the amount from 298 to 260. Finally, we reviewed the ACH file for 2 customers with $0.00. That customer is new to ACH and the Prenoted option was not checked. This prompted PowerManager to attempt to verify the ACH information by sending a $0.00 charge. I advised when entering new ACH information, to check the prenoted to prevent the $0.00 verification charge. Sandra can edit the ACH file to draft those account balances.

column2: Denise DeCoste] UB and meter reading for January were NOT finalized and already put in the February meter readings. I was working on the meter readings and billing for February. I uploaded all the February meter readings and computed charges. When I went to print the Utility bills for February, it would only print 1 customer, and she is a new lookup. Then I was told that I need to finalize the bills from January, which I did NOT do. I stopped working on February meter readings and utility bills, but I need to get the printed and emailed to customers ASAP. What is my next step? Please call me at 308-214-0514 or email me at dearmontdd@gmail.com
Susan Thomas] Called and left a message. This call is being transcribed. +130888933248:48 AM Hello, please leave a message after the tone.

Susan Thomas] +13082140514 10:15 AM Hi, if you record your name and reason for calling, I'll see if this person is available. 10:16 AM I'm sorry, this person is not available. If you would like to leave an additional message, please reply after the tone. Called again and left a message. Also tried the other number, no answer. 308-889-3324
Susan Thomas] Hi Denise, We attempted to reach you on both numbers but were unable to get through. Let us know once you are in the office so we can follow up. Susan

Susan Thomas] Denise returned the call and informed me that she will not be in the office until this evening. She asked me to walk her through the process. Since she had already computed charges for February, I advised her to uncompute the February charges, finalize January first, and then, when she is ready, recompute the February charges and print the bills for February.I walked her through each step, and she will reopen the ticket if she needs further assistance.
Denise DeCoste] I still need help!!The uncomputing February charges and finalizing January charges didn't work. Everything is all missed up now.
Susan Thomas] Called and left a message. This call is being transcribed. +130888933249:03 AM Hello, please leave a message after the tone.

Susan Thomas] I reached out to Denise this morning. We uncomputed the February charges, printed the January bill to PDF, and finalized January. I reviewed the meter tab for several accounts, and the February readings appear incorrect—January’s reading is missing, and the February reading looks like a combined value. I’m unsure what changes were made on Friday. Denise mentioned she is not in the office every day, but she can come in at 11:00 AM on Wednesday to review this further when Mitch Dahl is available.

Mitch Dahl] At this point, there are basically three options: Find and restore a backup dated prior to when the ticket was originally submitted and preferably, prior to when the February readings were entered. If need be, change the February readings back to the January readings and finalize the January bills. Then proceed with the February billling. Void all of the Printed 1/20, Due 2/10 bills, re-enter the January readings, compute, print, finalize the January bills. Enter the February readings and compute and print the February bills. Get R&D to delete all of the 1/15 charges and February meter readings. Start over with the January billing. Complete that process and then do the February billing.
Susan Thomas] Reached out and spoke with Denise, scheduled a call with Mitch Dahl tomorrow.

Mitch Dahl] Called Denise and connected remotely. Made a backup, then used the Table Utilities programs to delete all of the 1/15/2026 UB transactions and any meter readings dated beyond that date. Denise had already re-entered the January meter readings, so we then re-computed the January charges. Fixed some Compute Charges errors related to missing readings and computed charges again. Ran a Billing Stats report for January, found a couple of obvious mistakes and corrected those. The January Billing Stats is still off by a little. Denise will go through the report and try to identify the differences. Once those differences have been identified and corrected, she will then need to compute the January charges again, print the January bills and finalize the January bills. After that she can import the February meter readings and proceed with the February billing."

Tony Williams] Using the Layout Button on the Customer Screen Overview The Layout button on the Customer screen in Impresa allows users to customize how information is displayed. You can rearrange panels, save layouts for yourself, or create layouts that are shared across your entire utility. This article explains how layouts work, how to manage them safely, and what to expect when making changes. Layout Types Global Layouts (Shared) Available to all users within the same utility Any user can create, modify, or delete a global layout Changes take effect for everyone ⚠️ Important: Global layouts do not have permission controls. Changes or deletions impact all users immediately. My View (Personal Layout) Applies only to your user account Does not affect other users Recommended for personal preferences or testing changes Accessing Layout Options Open the Customer screen. Select the Layout button from the menu bar. Creating a Global Layout Open the Layout menu. Select New Global Layout. Rearrange panels or resize sections as needed. Select Save. What happens next: The layout is added to the list of available layouts It becomes the default layout It is available to all users 💡 Tip: After creating a new global layout, you may need to exit and reopen the Customer screen to see it display correctly. Updating an Existing Global Layout Select the global layout you want to change. Adjust the screen layout. The updated layout immediately applies to all users. Switching Between Layouts Use the layout list or checkboxes to switch between Global Layouts and My View. The last selected layout automatically loads the next time you open the Customer screen. Deleting a Global Layout Select the global layout. Select Delete. What to know: The layout is deleted for all users This action cannot be undone ⚠️ Use caution when deleting global layouts. Exporting a Layout You can save a copy of a layout to your computer as an image file. Select the layout you want to export. Select Export. Choose a folder location. Result: An image of the layout is saved to the selected folder. Importing a Layout Importing replaces the current layout with a previously saved layout image. Select My View or a global layout. Select Import. Choose the folder containing the saved layout. Select OK. Select Save to keep the imported layout. Important: Importing immediately overrides the current layout Importing behaves the same as manually changing the screen Known Limitations New global layouts may require closing and reopening the screen Exporting or importing layouts may cause temporary display issues In rare cases, the menu bar or Layout button may disappear and require a reset to defaults Use My View for personal layouts or experimentation Communicate before changing or deleting global layouts Export layouts before making major changes Summary Layouts allow flexible customization of the Customer screen in Impresa. Because global layouts affect all users and have no permission controls, they should be managed carefully. Using personal layouts for testing and shared layouts for standardization helps ensure a consistent and reliable experience."

Susan Thomas] Hello Scott, There are two main methods to generate a mailing list for utility customers: Steps to complete Option 1: Use the UB User Defined Reports Navigate to Utility Billing > Reports > User Defined Select ""Active Customer Mailing List Spreadsheet"" report Click OK to preview the report Use the export option to save as an Excel file for mail merge purposes Option 2: Print mailing labels directly Select ""Mailing Labels - Avery 5160 - By Zip Code"" report Print the labels on appropriate Avery label stock Alternative Option: Export from Customer Accounts Go to Utility Billing > Maintenance > Customer Accounts > Edit Use Filter to select only active customers (Status from 0 to 0) Right-click on any column to select which columns to display Use the Export option to save the data Please let us know if you need further assistance. Susan

LYNN GROSELY] I need to have the overtime pay amount displayed in box 14. From what I just learned the ""half"" amount in the time and a half can be deducted and needs to go in box 14. I will then need to resubmit corrected forms.
Joe Sheehan] We can either check box 14 in Earnings Overtime and add description FLSA OT or do a Employee History Report for calendar year 2025.

Susan Thomas] Hi Lynn, Please follow the steps. Option 1: Configure Overtime Earnings to Display in Box 14 The most direct solution is to modify the overtime earning code in the Payroll module to report in Box 14 of the W-2 form with an appropriate description. Option 2: Use Employee History Report An alternative approach is to run an Employee History Report for calendar year 2025 to capture the overtime information needed. Steps to complete Go to Payroll > Setup > Earnings/Deductions Maintenance Find and select your Overtime earning code Edit the earning and navigate to the W-2 reporting options Check the option to ""Print in Box 14"" for the overtime earning Enter an appropriate description for the Box 14 entry (such as ""FLSA OT"") Save your changes Test this by previewing a W-2 for an employee with overtime to ensure it appears correctly in Box 14 Once verified, you can print the corrected W-2 forms for submission Additional Information Box 14 on the W-2 form is designated for ""Other"" information that employers want to report to employees. This box is commonly used for reporting items that don't have a specific box elsewhere on the W-2 form. The W-2/W-4 page in the Employee Edit Form allows for configuration of what information appears on W-2 forms, and the earnings/deductions setup controls what values get reported in specific boxes.

JENNIFER WATERHOUSE] I am having an error message (see incorrect federal deduction error message below) after updating an employee's W-4 information. The top screen shot was what it was before the change. The bottom two are error messages after deleting out the $2000 in step 3 and checking the box in step 2 to match his new w4. Please help. Before the change- After the change & with the error message. I also tried entering zeros in the screen shot below this one and got the same outcome. Jennifer Jennifer Waterhouse City Clerk PO Box 504, 314 South 10th Ave Broken Bow, NE 68822 Office: 308-872-5831 Fax: 308-872-6885

Sreya Johny] Jennifer, This is a common issue when W-4 information is updated but the corresponding federal withholding deduction type is not properly aligned with the W-4 settings. The employee needs to have the correct federal withholding deduction type assigned that corresponds to their updated W-4 information with the Step 2 checkbox selected. Steps to Resolve Go to PR > Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions Use the Fed Update button to create the necessary federal withholding deduction types Since the employee has checked the Step 2 checkbox, they need to be assigned the deduction listed in the bottom section - 2020 or Later W-4 Step 2 Box Checked for Federal Married. If that is blank (as it is in the screen shot), you will need to add a new deduction. Go to the employee's record in Payroll and click Edit Navigate to the Deductions tab Check which federal withholding deduction is currently assigned to the employee If needed, click the + button to add a new deduction From the dropdown in the Deduct# column, select the appropriate federal withholding deduction that corresponds to ""Fed Married Step 2"" (or the appropriate filing status with Step 2 checked) Remove the previous federal withholding deduction if it's no longer applicable Save the changes Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

JENNIFER WATERHOUSE] I followed the steps and am getting the same message. Below is the screen shot of what was already listed in the 2020 or later section, so I did not have to enter anything. I updated the employee's deductions to use ""48. 2020 federal married"" instead of ""8. Federal married"" (see screenshot at the bottom) and I am getting the same error message. Also, I just realized that figures that are entered in the Federal income Tax Deduction #48 have not been updated for many years since we have not used this before. Do I need to update them to match all the figures in the #8 federal income tax deduction that we did at the start of the year? See below This has old figures from a few years ago... This was updated at the first of the year. Do I make #48 match these figures as well? Jennifer Jennifer Waterhouse City Clerk PO Box 504, 314 South 10th Ave Broken Bow, NE 68822 Office: 308-872-5831 Fax: 308-872-6885

Mitch Mullins] It appears the employee's active federal income tax deduction is correct. Deduction #48 should be used for married and step 2 box checked. I also noted the employee has a 2nd federal income tax deduction that is not checked to be computed and has $0.000 for the total. This will work, however, the preferred way when changing an employee's federal income tax deduction number is to replace the old with the new value instead of adding a new record for the change in federal income tax deduction number. This warning may be ignored without issue. Checking the ""Hide"" box on the no longer used federal deduction will prevent this warning from being displayed.

Sreya Johny] Jennifer, Instead of adding a second federal withholding deduction, replace the existing one (#8) with the appropriate ""Step 2"" deduction type (#48). If the employee has two federal income tax deductions listed, you should either: Delete the unused deduction, or Check the ""Hide"" box on the unused deduction to prevent the ""Incorrect Federal Deduction"" warning and click Save. This warning may be ignored without issue. There are different federal income tax tables depending on whether the Step 2 box is checked or not. Therefore, Deduction #8 and Deduction #48 are not supposed to be the same. Your tax tables should be up to date as long as you run the Federal Update option (PR > Maintenance > Earnings/Deductions > Fed Update > Update). Sreya
JENNIFER WATERHOUSE] This worked. Jennifer Jennifer Waterhouse City Clerk PO Box 504, 314 South 10th Ave Broken Bow, NE 68822 Office: 308-872-5831 Fax: 308-872-6885"

Susan Thomas] Hi Bev, I tried calling you earlier and left a message. I was informed that this will need to be completed manually. If you would like to remove the extra payments, please follow the steps below. To void unwanted payments in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, follow these steps: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the correct Posting Date for the adjusting entries (this date will be used for both Accounts Receivable and General Ledger transactions) Click Adjust Select the customer whose payment you want to void Go to the Payments tab In the Payment Selection Grid, click on the row containing the payment you wish to void (the selected payment will be highlighted) You can identify payments by their PayDate, RefNr, Amount, PayMethod, BatchNr, and Comment Click the Void button at the bottom right (only click ONCE) The selected payment will appear in the Adjustments section at the bottom of the screen Click Save to complete the void process Important Notes: Only click the Void button once to avoid the ""Records already exist"" error message Voiding a payment generates reversing entries that cancel out the original amount If Accounts Receivable is interfaced with General Ledger, reversing entries will also be posted to General Ledger This process should be used when a check does not clear the bank or when a payment is posted to an incorrect customer
