LESLIE NIELSEN] I am working with our auditors and they want me to close out the books as of 9/30/2024. I have never done this before and do not want to make a problem with everything!

Tony Williams] Leslie, I understand you need to close out your books as of 9/30/2024 for your auditors. Since this is your first time doing this process in PowerManager, I'll provide you with step-by-step instructions to help you complete this task safely. Before proceeding, here are the key steps for closing out your fiscal year: Make a complete backup of your dataThis is critical in case you need to restore your data for any reason. Run any year-end reports needed by your auditorsGenerate these reports before closing the year (Trial Balance, Income Statement, Balance Sheet, etc.) Ensure all transactions are properly entered and finalizedVerify all transactions for the fiscal year have been entered and finalized. Close the last month of your fiscal yearMake sure September 2024 has been closed in the system. Go to go to General Ledger > Processing > Close Period. Close the fiscal year From the PowerManager Main Menu, go to General Ledger > Processing > Close Year Click the Error Test button to check for any issues If the Error Test passes, the button will change to End Year Select your options for keeping history and click End Year to complete the process The Error Test will verify: All current year transactions are finalized The year is in balance (debits = credits) Valid close-to accounts are assigned The last month of the fiscal year has been closed Many customers also create a backup copy of their fiscal year data in a separate folder before closing. This preserves the data for the closed year while allowing you to continue working in the current year. If you encounter any errors during this process or would like assistance with the year-end close, please let us know and we can schedule a remote session to help you through it.
LESLIE NIELSEN] The clerk prior to me did do closeouts. I just have never done it since I have been working here. Does this change any of the steps? Also how do you recommend doing a backup? Leslie

Sreya Johny] Leslie, Steps to create a backup before closing the year: Go to File in PowerManager > Menu Editor > Company. Copy your company Data Location path. Open your Windows File Explorer and paste the path you copied to the address bar. Select all files in that location except the the Customer Bills PDF folder and copy them. Go back to the previous page and create a new folder for the backup. Make sure PowerManager is closed on all machines. Open the new folder and paste all the files. If any errors pop up during the copy process, please make sure all PowerManager related windows are closed on all machines, then click Try Again Once the copying is complete, copy the path of this backup folder from the address bar. Open PowerManager and go to File > Menu Editor > Company. Click ""+"" to insert new record for the backup. Enter a Company Name for the backup and paste the path you copied to the Data Location field. Click on the checkmark to save and then Close. Select Users from Menu Editor and click Copy. Choose the actual company file as the Source Company and select the backup company as the Destination Company. Emelia De Smet-Bacon] Pasting from Outlook: Good morning Shelly, This is correct! When the converter tool is processing the gWorks trial balances for each month, that’s exactly what it’s doing. You’re also correct about the addition or subtraction. If the number is positive, it should be entered as a debit. If it’s negative, it should be entered as a credit. This will update your account balances with the missing November transaction values. Make sure you dated these transactions for November 2025. Emelia, I sat down with it today and I think that I figured it out. There are 3 columns YTD BAL OCT YTD BAL NOV YTD Diff I entered the ‘YTD BAL NOV’ column numbers. If they were a positive number, I entered them into the Debit/Credit that the account type was. So, for example, if 100-01-1000 is a “D” (debit balance) account, I entered the positive number (IE: 25.00) as a debit and if it was a negative (IE: -25.00) I entered as a credit for that account. The same for the 2000 (Credit Balance), 3000 (Credit Balance), and 4000 (Debit Balance) accounts. I just really want to make sure that I entered the correct column and the correct way. I went back and looked at how the data conversion numbers were entered from the trial balance and I think that I am correct. I am going to double check the numbers tomorrow morning and just make sure I entered the right amounts. ShellyWieneke City Clerk-Treasurer City of Atkinson"

Susan Thomas] When hours are showing incorrectly in the Payroll Journal report, this is typically caused by duplicate or orphaned hour entries that aren't properly associated with earnings. Resolution Steps: Check for Non-Updated Entries in Void Paychecks Navigate to Void Paychecks Look for entries where ""updated and posted to GL"" columns are false If these entries are not the top line, delete them Check for Orphaned Hour Entries Use table grid to review PREmplPayHrs table Look for posted hours not associated with an earning Delete these orphaned records Reprocess the Payroll Go back to Post Hours and re-enter or verify the correct hours Calculate the amounts again Review the Journal report to verify hours are now displaying correctly If you continue to experience issues after following these steps, please provide more details about what specific hours are showing incorrectly so we can further assist you.

Mike Leicht] Due to snow, Becky is trying to estimate Cycle 1 using the process button on the readings maintenance. After she enters her book/seq numbers and clicks OK, she gets an error saying duplicate key. A line on the readings maintenance is created, but it has a blank count. Have you seen this error?

Chris Holloway] Hi Mike, Have the books been closed for these meters? The only time estimates work are when the meter read flag is unchecked. You may also check for meter models that are set to a multiplier of 0. This error has come up recently and the solution was to change any meter models to a multiplier of 1 that are currently set as 0, even if none of the meter models are attached to meters in the reading files. We have seen this error on the Verify Apply process, but makes sense that you may get it when trying to estimate as well.

Sreya Johny] Hi Shea, To interface Payroll with the General Ledger in PowerManager, please follow the steps below: 1. Open Payroll Setup Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Setup, then navigate to the GL Accounts tab. 2. Add Payroll GL Accounts Enter all General Ledger accounts that will be used in Payroll, such as: Payroll Cash Payroll Liability accounts (taxes and deductions) Payroll Expense accounts Note: If an account is not listed here, it will not appear as an option in the lookup sections. 3. Enable GL Integration In the GL Accounts tab, make sure “Use Actual GL Account Numbers” is checked so Payroll can interface with the General Ledger. 4. (Optional) Use Fill GL Account If you have multiple funds or departments: Enter the GL account for one fund Click Fill GL Account to automatically populate the same account for the other funds. 5. Set Up Cash Lookup Go to the Cash Lookup tab and select the GL account used for payroll net pay for each fund or department. This account represents the cash account payroll payments come from. 6. Set Up Liability Lookup In the Liability Lookup tab, assign the appropriate GL liability accounts for employee deductions and tax withholdings (e.g., Federal Tax, Social Security, Medicare, etc.). These accounts will track amounts withheld from employee paychecks. 7. Set Up Employer Expense Lookup In the Employer Expense Lookup tab, select the GL expense accounts used for employer payroll expenses such as employer Social Security, Medicare, retirement, or benefits. Note: Employers do not contribute to Federal or State Income Tax, so those accounts are not required here. Once these are configured and saved, Payroll will properly post transactions to the General Ledger. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Tony Williams] Sherna, We apologize that no one has responded to your inquiry about batch payments in Impresa. When processing payments made by customers on a weekend (January 31) and pulling those payments on the following business day (February 2), the payment date is determined by what you enter in the ""Batch payment date"" field during processing. By default, the system will use the current processing date (February 2), but you have the option to change this to the actual payment date (January 31). Any reports you generate will show the date you entered in this field. If you want the payments to be summarized with January payments, you should enter January 31 as the batch payment date when processing. If you enter February 2, they will be summarized with February payments. The payment date field is primarily for reporting purposes, so it's important to use the date that aligns with your accounting requirements.

Chris Peterson] When we return deposits to customer accounts, or anytime there is a credit on an account there is a penalty assessed. Can you tell me how to keep that from happening? I have attached a customer history so you can see what I am talking about.

Chris Peterson] When we return deposits to customer accounts, or anytime there is a credit on an account there is a penalty assessed. Can you tell me how to keep that from happening? I have attached a customer history so you can see what I am talking about.

Tony Williams] Chris, To prevent penalties from being assessed on credit balances when returning deposits to customer accounts, you'll need to adjust your Penalties & Fines setup in PowerManager. Here's how to fix this issue: From the PowerManager main menu, go to Utility Billing > Processing > Penalties & Fines Click the Setup button to access the penalty calculation parameters In the Penalties & Fines Setup form, go to the Options/Included Amounts tab Look for the option Exclude accounts with credit balances and check this box This setting will prevent the system from calculating penalties on any account with an overall credit balance If you need more specific control, you can also: Check the Exclude accounts with zero balance option Adjust the Minimum penalty amount to ensure small penalties aren't assessed Click Save to apply these changes For accounts that already have penalties incorrectly assessed on credits, you can remove these by: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Select the customer account Go to the Fines tab Enter a negative amount equal to the incorrect penalty Save the adjustment Additionally, the ""Transfer Credits"" utility can help prevent this issue by properly allocating credits: From the PowerManager main menu, select File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Select Transfer Credits from the menu options This will help distribute credits appropriately across service types before penalty calculations Let me know if you need any clarification or have questions about implementing these changes.

Sreya Johny] Chris, We've identified the solution to prevent penalties from being assessed on accounts with credit balances. You'll need to run the Transfer Credits process before calculating penalties each time. When you return deposits or have credits on customer accounts, these credits need to be properly applied to any amounts owed before penalty calculations occur. The Transfer Credits process handles this by: Transferring credits from one service type to another Applying credits to outstanding balances Ensuring accounts with overall credit balances aren't penalized Steps to Run the Transfer Credits Process From the PowerManager main menu, go to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Click File > Select Transfer Credits from the menu options Enter the Payment Date and Posting Date. The ""Type of Payment"" will automatically be set to ""Transfer"" Click the CR Transfer button and select all customers When prompted ""Do you want to manually save each credit transfer payment?"": Select ""Yes"" if you want to review each customer's transfers before saving Select ""No"" to have the system automatically complete all transfers If you select YES, then the payment entry form will be displayed for each customer that has both credits and arrears. Click the “Save” button to post the transfer payment and continue searching for the next customer with both credit and arrears balances owed Click the “Cancel” button to not post the transfer payment. You will then be asked if you wish to continue searching for the next customer with credits and arrears balances owed Note: Always run the Transfer Credits process just prior to calculating penalty amounts. This sequence is critical to ensure proper credit application before penalties are assessed. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Sreya Johny] Chris, We've identified the solution to prevent penalties from being assessed on accounts with credit balances. You'll need to run the Transfer Credits process before calculating penalties each time. When you return deposits or have credits on customer accounts, these credits need to be properly applied to any amounts owed before penalty calculations occur. The Transfer Credits process handles this by: Transferring credits from one service type to another Applying credits to outstanding balances Ensuring accounts with overall credit balances aren't penalized Steps to Run the Transfer Credits Process From the PowerManager main menu, go to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Click File > Select Transfer Credits from the menu options Enter the Payment Date and Posting Date. The ""Type of Payment"" will automatically be set to ""Transfer"" Click the CR Transfer button and select all customers When prompted ""Do you want to manually save each credit transfer payment?"": Select ""Yes"" if you want to review each customer's transfers before saving Select ""No"" to have the system automatically complete all transfers If you select YES, then the payment entry form will be displayed for each customer that has both credits and arrears. Click the “Save” button to post the transfer payment and continue searching for the next customer with both credit and arrears balances owed Click the “Cancel” button to not post the transfer payment. You will then be asked if you wish to continue searching for the next customer with credits and arrears balances owed Note: Always run the Transfer Credits process just prior to calculating penalty amounts. This sequence is critical to ensure proper credit application before penalties are assessed. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Teresa Akeson] I ran bills this morning, and then did the finalize. I have 54 pages of errors; it's basically all the accounts. I do not understand what has happened. I also need this process further explained.I printed bills prior to finalizing like I have been told to do. Does this issue affect the printed bills in any way?
Ben Aplin] Joe, could you please take a look at this one.

Joe Sheehan] Issue Teresa is experiencing errors during the bill finalization process in PowerManager Utility Billing. She reports having 54 pages of errors affecting essentially all accounts after running bills and attempting to finalize them. She's concerned about whether this issue impacts the printed bills and needs clarification on the finalization process. Analysis Based on the related tickets, this appears to be a common issue where the finalization process encounters errors related to either transactions not being marked as billed or meter readings not being marked as billed. The high volume of errors (54 pages) suggests a systematic issue rather than individual account problems. Proposed Solution The most likely cause is that the meter readings or transactions haven't been properly marked as billed in the system. This typically happens when bills are printed but the system doesn't register them as printed, or when there's an interruption in the finalization process (like in ticket #105775 where a power outage occurred). Steps to Complete 1. First, verify if the bills were actually printed correctly before attempting to finalize. Teresa mentioned she did print the bills before finalizing, which is the correct procedure. 2. Check the specific error messages in the 54 pages. Based on similar tickets, these are likely ""Transaction not marked as billed"" or ""Meter readings not marked as billed"" errors. 3. For transactions not marked as billed: Manually mark these transactions as billed in the system. 4. For meter readings not marked as billed: These can be manually marked as billed through the system or via a database query if there are too many to handle individually. 5. After addressing the errors, run the finalization process again to confirm the issues are resolved. 6. Regarding Teresa's question about whether this affects printed bills - assure her that if the bills were printed correctly before the finalization attempt, they should be accurate and can still be distributed to customers. The finalization errors don't invalidate already printed bills. Key People That May Be Able to Assist Based on the related tickets, these customer support agents have experience solving similar finalization errors: Susan Thomas Mitch Dahl Mitch Mullins Additional Information I would recommend scheduling a follow-up training session with Teresa to review the finalization process. The related document ""Webinar - Understanding the finalize bills process"" could be shared with her for additional guidance. This will help prevent similar issues in future billing cycles. Referenced Documents 105653: Finalizing bills (error) 94127: From Voicemail 107697: error on finalization of bills 105775: Finalize Errors 93866: Piggott - From voicemail

Joe Sheehan] I'll help you with information on how to mark transactions as billed in the PM - Utility Billing system. Let me search our knowledge base for the specific procedure.Based on my research, I can provide instructions for marking transactions as billed in the PowerManager Utility Billing system: How to Mark Transactions as Not Billed to Billed To mark transactions that are not currently marked as billed in the PowerManager Utility Billing system: Navigate to Utility Billing > Processing > Compute Charges For the specific customers with unbilled transactions: Select the customer(s) from the customer selection form Ensure the ""Billable"" option is checked for the relevant accounts Click ""Compute"" to calculate the charges After computing charges, verify the transactions in the customer history to confirm they are now properly marked as billed For commercial accounts specifically: Check that the ""Billable"" option is selected for commercial electric accounts This is a common issue that can prevent transactions from being marked as billed For multiple customers with the same issue: Add all affected customers to the Customer List Use batch processing to compute charges for all of them together This will mark all their transactions as billed at once For inactive customers with zero usage: These can also be marked as billed through the same process Select these customers and compute charges to update their status If you need further assistance with specific accounts or have additional questions about this process, please let me know.

This means they are either selecting the UB Payments Cash Register item when entering these payments or they have a Cash Register item for Deposits with the Destination of Utility Billing. Don't use the UB Payments CR item to post payments received for service deposits and/or set the Destination for the Deposits CR item to Other. They will need to void the payments in UB-Adjustments that have been recorded for service deposits.

Susan Thomas] Hi Hayley, please follow the steps below to achieve the correct outcome. Issue Identified When payments for service deposits are being entered into Cash Register, they are being applied to the Utility Billing (UB) customer account.This means one of the following is occurring: The UB Payments Cash Register item is being selected when entering these deposit payments, or The Cash Register item used for deposits has its Destination set to Utility Billing. Because of this, the deposit payments are being sent into UB as regular customer payments instead of being recorded strictly as deposits. Required Actions 1. Stop Using the UB Payments CR Item for Service Deposits Do not select the UB Payments Cash Register item when entering service deposit payments. This item is intended only for posting regular customer payments into UB. 2. Update the Deposits Cash Register Item Go to the Cash Register setup for the item used to record service deposits. Change the Destination to Other, not Utility Billing.This will prevent deposit payments from being posted to the UB customer account. 3. Void Incorrect Payments in Utility Billing Payments that were incorrectly posted to UB as a result of this setup need to be corrected. Go to UB → Adjustments. Void the payments that were incorrectly recorded for service deposits. Re‑enter the payments correctly in Cash Register using the updated CR item.

Joe Sheehan] Mikey, See below for step-by-step instructions on how to handle this. Remember: if the paycheck hasn't been finalized yet there's no need to void first run PR Data File Maintenance on the employee. Joe Issue A new employee, Jesus Martinez, has a direct deposit issue where the payment did not go through. The customer is waiting on confirmation from the employee's bank, but needs instructions on how to void the direct deposit and issue a paper check today if necessary. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and similar tickets, there are clear steps to void a direct deposit payment and issue a paper check instead. Since the payroll was likely already processed but not finalized yet (as this is happening on the last day of the month), we can use the appropriate method to handle this situation. Steps to complete If the payroll has NOT been finalized yet: Go to File > Data File Maintenance > PR Data File Maintenance Select ""Un-print Non-updated Payroll Checks"" option under File Select Jesus Martinez from the employee list Once the direct deposit has been un-printed: Go to Payroll > Processing > Print Checks Select ""Print Checks"" as the Payment Method (instead of ACH) Enter the appropriate Check Date, Pay Period Start Date, and Pay Period Ending Date Click Next or Select Employees Select only Jesus Martinez from the employee list Select the Bank Account to use, enter the Starting Check# and select the Check Form Click Next Verify the information is correct and click Print to print the paper check Confirm the check printed correctly when prompted If the payroll HAS been finalized: Go to Payroll > Processing > Void Paychecks Select Jesus Martinez Void the direct deposit payment Process the payroll again for that employee using the same date Follow the Print Checks process to issue a paper check

Sachin Yadav] Morning Tim, Here is what our DEV found for the issue that occurred, From DEV - The first guess is that if somebody reverses a payment that is still in open transactions, it may use the username of the user who currently holds the open payment. No, the code looks like it registers the reverse payment on the user doing the reverse. And there is no action that icloud user would do to make a meter change out by the IC processes. The iCloud user is an actual user. You may check to see which AD user has the name of the iCloud user. could be somebody actually logged in as the AD user associated with that name. not likely but possible. They probably should have the AD user name set to something that does not exist It is also possible that if a user lost network connection and the IC process made a payment and registered itself as that user in sa_user_spid, then the actual user reconnected.... sa_user_spid has associated the spid with iClouduser and a lot of what they do could wind up being identified as iCloudUser rather than the actual user, but most likely they should have received a ""spid change error"" and be kicked out at some point.... and you did not mention any of that. Tyler Silvernail] Hi Christ, Rose, What is your goal with this account? Do you want it to be: Active and bill? Then set the Bill Code to 'Normal Billing'. It will bill moving forward. Active and Not Bill? Leave it as is and the account will remain active but will not be included in calculations until you set the Bill code to Normal (seasonal). Inactive and wont bill? Then add an end date or apply a move out action and the next time you bill the account will be flagged and turned inactive after billing. Active and Bill one last time? Do a move out action and appl y an end date, flip the billing code to Normal. This will bill the account but will then flag it as inactive after billing. As you guys know... The bill code is just an on off switch if you want the account to be included or excluded in calculations. If you apply an end data to the account, the next time you run billing the system is going to look at that and either inactive the account (if in the past) or keep it open for billing (if NULL). Once inactive the bill code isnt even looked at. Think of it this way: Bill Code = Normal (dude is living in the house and will get billed) Bill Code = Do Not Bill (dude lives in the house, but he's not there right now) Account Active = (dude lives in the house and may or may not be billed based on bill code) Account Inactive = (dude moved out of the house and is not coming back that you are aware of) Account inactive will only set itself IF THERE IS AN END DATE APPLIED and it goes through a billing cycle. So again, you need to ask yourselves what do you want to do with this account because I don't know what you want to do with it. 🙂 T Good morning, Tyler I have attached a screenshot of the account, which shows it is Inactive under the Premise location but shows Active at the top, next to the account number. We have disconnected the meter and put in the final read. Please advise. Christi Mitchell Customer Service Representative Naknek Electric Association PO Box 118 Naknek, AK 99633 1-(907)-246-4261 christim@naknekelectric.com"

column2: Tyler Silvernail] Hi Tyler, Is there a way for us to open accounts back up that have already been closed? What would you recommend at this point if an account has already been closed but we are wanting monthly reads on it. Cedalia Tryan Clerk 1 Burney Water District ctryan@burneywater.org

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Cedalia, You can, but when a move out happens and the account goes through a billing cycle there are a number of flags that are swapped on the account. Charge end dates, name end dates etc. So we can always 'turn them back on' but then BM will assume that you know that someone is living at that premise but are just not getting billed. If this is something you want we can explore different options for you to go through and reactivate accounts. T

Tyler Silvernail] Meeting recap: Burney wants to track account consumption even after move out of the tenant. The way for BillMaster to do this is to have the account active with an installed meter. In order to have these two conditions met, the account has to have an active name. They want to track but not bill. So I suggested they move a tenant in with 'Vacant' listed in the name then set a Bill Code flag = Vacant Tracking. The bill code will allow the account to stay open however skip billing. They can then see if consumption is being used via the import/export processes. Set final read on old tenant move old tenant out (if not already done) Move new tenant in set bill code = 'Vacant Tracking' Tracking will be done via a bill code report. I will attach the bill code report to the task menu for easy access (meter reading) Bill Code Report: Account Service Address Name Meter # Consumption for current bill period quoted 1 hour for report

Tyler Silvernail] /* METER READINGS By BILL CODE (Report #139) 1/30/2026 tfs Burney has a number of accounts (30-60?) that dare 'vacant' but still consume water. They wanted to track these. The account is set to active with an installed meter, hover the bill code is set to 'Vacant Tracking' by staff so they can track these accounts */ /* 45 159 1B266C314F1B266B3253 1B45 */SELECT 'Meter Readings by Bill Code' UNION ALLSELECT 'Printed ' + CONVERT(varchar(25), GETDATE(), 100) + ' Page: ' GO /* _WIDTH5_Route 1*/SELECT PTntDefaultBillCode AS '_WIDTH20_PTntDefaultBillCode', PMRdvfFmtPremTenant AS '_WIDTH15_Account #', AddrvfFullAddress AS '_WIDTH35_Service Address', MetrMeterNumber AS '_WIDTH12_Meter Num', PMRdStartReading AS '_WIDTH10_NOSUB_Begin Read', PMRdEndReading AS '_WIDTH10_NOSUB_End Read', PMRdvfAdjConsumption AS '_WIDTH10_Adj Cons', CONVERT(char(10), PMRdStartDate, 101) AS 'Start Date', CONVERT(char(10), PMRdEndDate , 101) AS 'End Date'FROM PremiseTenantsJOIN PremiseMeterReadings ON PMrdPremiseID = PTNTPremiseiD AND PMRdTenantCounter = PTNtTenantCounterJOIN Premises ON PMRdPremiseID = PremPremiseIDJOIN BillingGroups ON PremBillingGroup = BGrpBillingGroupJOIN Addresses ON PremAddressID = AddrAddressIDJOIN PremiseMeters ON PMRdPremiseID = PMtrPremiseID AND PMRdMeterID = PMtrMeterIDJOIN Meters ON PMRdMeterID = MetrMeterIDJOIN MeterReadCodes ON PMRdReadCode = XXMRMeterReadCodeJOIN Routes ON PMtrRouteBook = RoutRouteNumberWHERE PtntDefaultBillCode IN (?01)AND PMRdBillGroup = BGrpBillingGroupAND PMRdBillPeriod = BGrpBillingPeriodORDER BY RoutDescription, PMtrRoutePage, MetrMeterKind

Joe Sheehan] Issue Ashley from the City of Ponca is trying to combine multiple customer accounts that belong to the same customer but have separate accounts for the same service address. She has attempted several methods without success and is requesting guidance on how to properly combine these accounts. Proposed Solution Based on the related tickets, there are two possible solutions depending on the specific situation: 1. If the accounts are for the same service location: You can merge the accounts using the UB Data File Maintenance tool. 2. If the accounts are for different service locations: Complete merging is not recommended as it can cause confusion in billing. However, you could perform a move-out on one account and then do a move-in selecting the existing customer for the other location. Steps to Complete (for same service location) 1. Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance 2. Select File > Merge Customers 3. Select the source customer account (the one you want to merge from) 4. Select the destination customer account (the one you want to keep) 5. Complete the merge process Important Considerations 1. Accounts can only be fully merged if they are at the same service location 2. If attempting to combine accounts from different locations, be aware that the customer will receive one bill and may not be able to distinguish which charges are for which location 3. For accounts at different locations that need to be billed together, you'll need to decide if multiple meters on one account will be billed with individual rates or combined under a single rate 4. If you're in the middle of a billing cycle, you may want to wait until bills are finalized before moving meters to different locations Customer Support Agents Who Have Solved Similar Issues 1. Susan Thomas 2. Mitch Dahl 3. Mitch Mullins 4. Tyler Silvernail Referenced Documents 102968: combining two accounts 96537: change name on account 90648: Move In's 102289: Merge accounts 296 90384: Preston - How to combine multiple accounts into one.

Joe Sheehan] Ashley, I understand you're looking to combine multiple customer accounts that belong to the same customer but have separate accounts for the same service address. There are two approaches depending on your specific situation: For accounts at the same service location: Navigate to File > Data File Maintenance > UB Data File Maintenance Select File > Merge Customers Select the source customer account (the one you want to merge from) Select the destination customer account (the one you want to keep) Complete the merge process Important considerations: Accounts can only be fully merged if they are at the same service location If you're in the middle of a billing cycle, you may want to wait until bills are finalized before merging accounts The merge process cannot be undone, so I recommend backing up your data first If the accounts are for different service locations, complete merging is not recommended as it can cause confusion in billing. In that case, you could perform a move-out on one account and then do a move-in selecting the existing customer for the other location. Let me know if you need any clarification or have questions about this process. Joe

JENNIFER PELLATZ] I have questions.1. I would like to know if in Power Manager there is a way for the system to automatically change rates on the electric meters. Normally this is something that I have to manually do. Being told by another user that the system automatically changes the rate just like changing the winter/summer rates.2. I need to speak about the Demand meter electric rates and the Tantalus system. We audit our demand meters each year. I would like to talk about changing the meters back and forth between reading the demand and not reading the demand.Maybe we can talk about these issues when you call about the AR penalty issue.
Ben Aplin] Hello - we are reviewing your ticket and will get back to you shortly.

Mitch Mullins] There is not a method for automatically changing rates. Rates will need to be manually edited to update their values. You can change a meter's read number max value from 2 to 1 to change a demand meter to a non-demand meter. Change the read number max value from 1 to 2 to change a non-demand meter to a demand meter. You may also need to change between demand and non-demand rates when changing the read number max value.

Sreya Johny] Hi Jennifer, Apologies for the delayed response! 1. There isn’t a way to automatically change rates. The rates will need to be manually edited to update their values. 2. You can change a meter from demand to non-demand by updating the Read Number Max value from 2 to 1 (under UB > Maintenance > Equipment > Meter > Edit the meter that needs to be changed). To change a meter from non-demand to demand, update the value from 1 to 2. You may also need to switch between demand and non-demand rates when changing the Read Number Max value. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Chris Peterson] So I can change the meter reading and adjust the bill after it has been finalized? Chris Peterson City of Imperial Billing Clerk

Sreya Johny] Hi Chris, To void and adjust a finalized bill, go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments. Enter the original Posting Date and click Adjust. Select the customer account, then go to the Bills tab and choose the bill that needs to be voided. Click the Void button on the right. Once the bill appears in the Adjustments section at the bottom, go to the Re-Bill tab. Make the necessary changes to the readings, check the ""Post readings and calculate charges"" box, and then Save. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

Sreya Johny] Spoke with Nicole. The default amount for the Flat Sewer component should be $16, but it appears it was changed to $0 last year. It should have a base rate of $16, a CWSRF fee of $41, and then the add-on rate. She doesn’t believe it’s set up correctly. On the attached account, the add-on amount looks doubled.

Mitch Dahl] We leave the Default Amount for the Flat Sewer component set at $0.00. When we use the Calculate Customer Amounts option under Maintenance-Rate to calculate the new sewer charge we incorporate the base $16 into the calculations. The customer's Add-On Amount is the total of their sewer charge (base charge + usage fee). The Sewer CWSRF Fee is a separate charge that isn't involved in the calculation of the new sewer charges.

Sreya Johny] Good morning Nicole, Mitch reviewed this and confirmed that the Default Amount for the Flat Sewer component should remain set to $0.00. When using the Calculate Customer Amounts option under Maintenance > Rate to calculate the new sewer charge, we incorporate the base $16 into the calculations. The customer’s Add-On Amount represents the total sewer charge (base charge + usage fee). The Sewer CWSRF Fee is a separate charge and isn’t included in the sewer charge calculation. Please let us know if you have any questions. Sreya

column1: 20994893,"Error: Run setup, enter missing liability lookup values, and refigure distribution","[2026-01-28 03:58
column2: Ben Aplin] # Payroll Error: ""Run setup, enter missing liability lookup values, and refigure distribution"" ## **Issue Summary**Users receive the error message ""Run setup, enter missing liability lookup values, and refigure distribution"" when processing payroll updates or finalizing payroll periods. ## **Symptoms**- Error appears in payroll processing status window- Payroll amounts cannot be finalized or posted to GL- Error specifically occurs under ""Deduction Errors"" or ""Employer Contribution Liability Errors"" sections- Employees have active earnings distributions but payroll processing fails ## **Root Cause**The system cannot find GL account assignments for liability deductions because: 1. **Employee earnings** are distributed to specific department-fund GL accounts (e.g., `02-02-5000`, `02-03-5000`)2. **System requires matching liability lookups** with corresponding department-fund patterns (e.g., `02-02-0000`, `02-03-0000`)3. **Liability lookup entries exist** in the setup tables but have **no GL account assigned**4. **During payroll processing**, the system cannot map deduction liabilities to actual GL accounts ## **Technical Details**- **Affected Tables**: `PRGLLiab` (Employee Deduction Liability), `PRGLECLiab` (Employer Contribution Liability)- **Key Fields**: `LookupAcct` (department-fund pattern), `PRGLAcctsKey` (GL account assignment)- **Process Flow**: Employee earnings GL accounts → Extract department-fund pattern → Match to liability lookup → Assign GL account ## **Resolution Steps** ### **Step 1: Identify Specific Missing Assignments**Run this query to find **only the employees and distributions** causing the current error:```sqlSELECT DISTINCT e.IdNr, a.Name as EmployeeName, gla.FullAcct as EarningGLAccount, liab.LookupAcct, liab.PREDKey as DeductionKey, ed.Description as DeductionDescription, 'Missing GL Account Assignment' as IssueFROM PREmpl e INNER JOIN SCAddress a ON a.""Key"" = e.SCAddressKey INNER JOIN PREmplXEarn exe ON exe.PREmplKey = e.PREmplKey INNER JOIN PREmplPctDist pct ON pct.PREmplXEarnKey = exe.PREmplXEarnKey INNER JOIN PRGLAccts gla ON gla.PRGLAcctsKey = pct.PRGLAcctsKey INNER JOIN PRGLLiab liab ON LEFT(liab.LookupAcct, 6) = LEFT(gla.FullAcct, 6) INNER JOIN PRED ed ON ed.PREDKey = liab.PREDKey LEFT JOIN PRGLAccts liabGL ON liabGL.PRGLAcctsKey = liab.PRGLAcctsKeyWHERE liabGL.GLAcctKey IS NULL;``` ### **Step 1: Focus Only on Error-Referenced Distributions****IMPORTANT**: Only update the specific **LookupAcct** values and **deduction types** shown in your query results. Do not assign GL accounts to other liability lookups that are not currently being used. ### **Step 2: Access Payroll Setup**1. Go to **Payroll** → **Setup**2. Click the **Liability Lookup** tab3. Select the specific deduction type identified in Step 1 (e.g., Social Security, Medicare) ### **Step 3: Assign GL Accounts for Identified Distributions Only**1. Locate **only the LookupAcct entries** that exactly match those from Step 12. For each entry showing in your error results with a blank **GL Acct#** field: - Click in the **GL Acct#** field - Select the appropriate liability GL account number - Save the assignment3. **Do not assign GL accounts** to other LookupAcct entries that weren't in your errors ### **Step 4: Repeat for Employer Contributions (If Applicable)**1. If the error also mentions ""Employer Contribution Liability Errors"": - Click the **Employer Liability Lookup** tab - Repeat the process for **only the specific distributions** identified in Step 1 ## **Prevention**- **Before adding new employees**: Ensure their specific department-fund combinations have corresponding liability lookup GL accounts assigned- **When creating new departments/funds**: Set up liability lookups only for combinations that will be actively used- **Regular maintenance**: Run the diagnostic query to identify missing assignments for active employee distributions only ## **Related Information**- **Error Types**: This same process applies to ""Employer Contribution Liability Errors"" and ""Employee Deduction Liability"" errors- **Account Patterns**: LookupAcct uses first two components (department-fund) of earnings GL accounts plus ""-0000"" for liabilities- **Targeted Approach**: Only assign GL accounts to liability lookups that are actively being used by current employee distributions ## **Keywords**payroll, liability, lookup, GL account, distribution, deduction, employer contribution, setup, missing ---"

Sachin Yadav] Rate code setup Setup Income Center and Rates tab Select the service and click the Details button on the right side of the screen. To create a new rate code, click the Add Rate button at the bottom of the screen. Near the top, enter an up to 3 character name for this rate and to the right put in a description of what the rate is for and once done click the Save button. Make sure the rate is highlighted in the Code and Description box, on the left, so we are editing the correct rate. If this rate is a flat rate or a tiered rate with a minimum step, make sure the Step 1 is a Minimum checkbox is checked. Near the bottom left, click the Add Step button. To the left a white like will appear with a separator between the Units section and Charge section. In the left side enter in the usage for this step of the rate code. Then on the right side enter in the chare for this step. The first step on a tiered rate or a flat rate might be stated as for the first 2000 units the charge will be $20. So for that step would look like this: If there is no usage allotted for the first step it would just be 0 For the rest of the steps, if any, the usage value will be the usage for that step or part of the rate. Often an ordinance or rate table might say something like, from 2001 to 10000 units used will be charged at 2.75 per 1000 units. A common mistake is to put 10000 for the usage on this step. That is wrong. This step is 8000 units total (10000 – 2000) as stated in the ordinance. Make sure you set it up in this manner or the rate will not calculate correctly. Repeat this for the rest of the steps until you get to the step that usually says something like for everything over that $3.00. In this case the Units box would be a + to signify everything about the last step. So a completed rate might look like as follows: This rate might be stated as: Up to first 2000 units will be charged at $20. From 2001 to 10000 is $2.75 per 1000/units. From 10001 to 20000 units is $3.00 per 1000 units. Anything over 20000 units is $3.75 per 1000 units.

cust] Missed Chat RequestFirst Name:ROXANNELast Name:MEYEREmail:hoopercityclerk@hooper.ne.govQuestion:please send instructions for estimating water meter reads to the prior month's read and calculating a bill

ROXANNE MEYER] i set up estimating water meter reads to pull previous month read over. it wants to average the last 3 months instead of just pulling last months read over for billing. i refreshed all and calculated all but i am missing a step to only pull last month's read over.

Susan Thomas] I reached out to Roxanne and walked her through the steps. She mentioned that the bills have already been sent out, but she will try this process next month. If she needs assistance, she will reach out. This issue can be resolved by changing the meter reading estimation settings to use only the previous month's reading instead of a 3-month average. This is done through the Estimated Reading Setup configuration. Steps to complete Access the Estimated Reading Setup in the Utility Billing EZ module Change the ""months of history to use"" setting from the current value (likely set to 3) to 1 Click the ""Refresh"" button to apply these changes Return to meter reading entry and select ""Estimate All Readings"" again The system should now pull just the previous month's readings instead of using a 3-month average Verify the estimated readings are correct before proceeding with billing"

Julie Funk] Hello,We sent out a bill last week to a customer that resulted in a request for a leak check. We went out today to check and found it was manually read and was read 10000 gallons more than the current read.We want to revise the reading and reprint the bill.What is the process we should take to successfully send out a corrected bill to the customer?

Tony Williams] Julie, Instead of reprinting bills, Void the Bill run (and any additional Bill runs made for this) and start over. Print Bills again. This will properly maintain the service dates and ensure charges appear in the correct column. Additional Information After a bill run is created, all new transactions are marked with a flag that shows it was included in this bill run and it also puts the due dates on each transaction in the database. This is important to remember, because if you re-read an account after the bill is printed, then the new charges MUST go through another bill run to mark new transactions with the correct due date, etc.

Megan Bunch] printing bills and it keeps putting them in a different service date and making everything go into the balance forward column instead of current charges area. i did have to go back and reprint them so when it is printing in the second round it wont let me chang the dates.

Tony Williams] Megan, Root Cause Based on our documentation and related tickets, this appears to be an issue with how Impresa handles bill reprinting. When bills are reprinted, the system is using the current date instead of preserving the original service date. Once a notice has been created with any type of notice, creating another new bill run for the same account will print all balances as PREVIOUS BALANCES."" This explains why the charges are appearing in the balance forward column instead of current charges. Reprinting isn't the Proposed Solution Instead of creating a new bill run when reprinting bills, Void the Bill run (and any additional Bill runs made for this) and start over. Print Bills again. This will properly maintain the service dates and ensure charges appear in the correct column. Additional Information After a bill run is created, all new transactions are marked with a flag that shows it was included in this bill run and it also puts the due dates on each transaction in the database. This is important to remember, because if you re-read an account after the bill is printed, then the new charges MUST go through another bill run (or revised bill run) to mark new transactions with the correct due date, etc.

Ben Aplin] Hi Tricia - I've updated the severity of your ticket to an S1 since you have an issue blocking the completion of a critical business process that needs to be completed very shortly. We'll be in touch shortly. Please do select an S1 when creating tickets if it something that needs to be completed immediately.

Mitch Mullins] We would want to make sure the prior pay period has been updated. This can be verified by using the void paychecks application and making note of the Updateed colum. If false then the prior pay period needs to be updated. It's also possible that the prior pay period was updated and the option to carry forward posted hours from the prior pay period is checked. If the posting date didn't get changed then the carry forward hours would look the same as the prior pay period.

Susan Thomas] Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and similar tickets, this appears to be related to the ""Use prior posted hours"" setting in the Post Hours Setup form. When this option is checked, the system uses hours from the previous pay period as default values for the current pay period. Tricia needs to uncheck this option when starting a new payroll cycle. Steps to complete Go to Payroll/Processing menu and select ""Post Hours"" On the Post Hours Setup form, make sure the ""Use prior posted hours"" checkbox is UNCHECKED Enter the correct posting date for the current payroll period Click either ""Post"" (for daily entries) or ""Easy Post"" (for posting total hours to a single day) based on your preference Select the employees to process and enter the new hours without the old hours appearing as defaults Complete the remaining payroll process steps (Calculate, Edit, Print Checks, Update)

Susan Thomas] Tricia had six employees showing ""old hours"". We checked in Void Paychecks and all of them had “updated = false,” so we updated them. After updating the employees, she tried to post the hours for January 26, but the PR fields were grayed out and she couldn’t make any edits. We then tried updating the January 16 payroll, but we ran into multiple errors. We attempted to update each employee again, but the same errors kept appearing. We also went into Maintenance, looked up the missing records, and added the ones she recognized. However, even after entering the deduction GL accounts from the error messages, the same errors still came up.

Emelia De Smet-Bacon] AP history is not converted with PM data conversions. We talk about this specifically throughout the project. Customers need to enter any AP history that is outstanding for bank rec purposes. 1099s from gWorks transactions should be produced with gWorks. 1099s from PM should be produced with PM. Anything outstanding from in between the transition should be entered in PM so checks can be recorded, bank rec can be done, GL stays in balance, etc.

Redistributions should have a label of Other Redistributions on the reports. it should not just say Other. As I stated on the phone, Most all processes have a label to le tyou know what has happened. Jody Baughman] Issued this customer a refund deposit - via check that was mailed not deducted from the account. The account has a discount of $150.00 for the deposit refund which isn't correct. I have been in adjustments multiple times to add this $150.00 back to get the correct account balance of $286.41 but isn't not working. Attached is a copy of the account history. 1/21/26 Deposit account deduction of $150.00 is not correct.

Susan Thomas] Hi Jodi, please follow the steps. If you need additional assistance, please reach out. Proposed Solution We need to correct this by reversing the incorrect deposit transaction and then properly processing the refund using the correct method. Steps to complete Enter a negative $150 deposit transaction to cancel out the incorrect entry. This can be done through UB - Processing - Adjustments by selecting the customer account and entering a negative deposit transaction in the Adjustments section. Once the incorrect entry is canceled, properly process the deposit refund by going to UB - Processing - Deposits. In the Deposits screen, select ""Refunded Deposit"" as the transaction type and choose ""Print a check"" option (not ""Apply to bill"") since the refund was issued via check. Verify the account balance is now correctly showing $286.41 by reviewing the Customer History report. If the General Ledger becomes out of balance after making these corrections, you may need to make adjusting entries in the GL to reconcile the accounts.

Kristina Bautista] Hello, I have a question regarding the process. If a cycle is in current updated status and we upload new meter reads into BillMaster, will those new reads be locked into the system once the cycle is closed? Or, when we close the cycle, will only the accounts that received a full update calculation be finalized, while the newly uploaded reads remain on the account as unbilled transactions? Kristina Bautista Supervisor of Data Services Office (951) 867-4643 Cell (951) 390-1931 This communication contains information that may be

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Kristina, I hope you had a great weekend. If you run an update, then add a read, then close, the system will lock that read in the closed bill period but not calculate it. If you want to add a read you'll need to cancel the update, add the read then re-run your update. If you want the reads in the NEXT bill period, you'll have to import them after close.. If your goal was to add reads early, the system will put them in the most current bill period since you are creating the next bill period during close. I hope this makes sense.T

Kristina Bautista] Hi Tyler, I’m getting over a cold so I am starting to feel better. To clarify, if we add a meter read prior to closing the cycle, will the meter read that we added get locked into the system? Meaning will that read still generate in any future outsource generated or will the meter read be modifiable?

Tyler Silvernail] Hi Kristina, I hope you are feeling better. So if you add a read prior to closing, yes, it will be placed into the bill period it was lclosed in and not allow you to freely edit it. You would need to use the auto adjust feature or the Service Order wizard to edit the reads after closing. But as stated, if there is a rate attached to it it will not be apart of the balance if you add after update. Examples: 1 Add Read 2 Full Update 3 Close *BM will calculate the read * rate and update balance 1 Full Update 2 Add Read *BM has record of the read and will place it in the closed bill period however the rate will not be calculated as it was added AFTER you asked BM to calculate balances 3 Cancel Update 4 Redo Full update 5 Close The consumption will be there (in the closed bill period) but will not be accounted for in financials. T
Tyler Silvernail] Did this answer your question?
Kristina Bautista] Yes.
Tyler Silvernail] Have a great weekend!T
Kristina Bautista] Yes. Have a great day!"

Mitch Dahl] There is no place on the W-2 for Gross Wages. Box 1 is the wages subject to Federal Income Tax withholding, Box 3 is the wages subject to the Social Security withholding and Box 3 is the wages subject to the Medicare withholding. These wages will differ from the Gross wages by the amount of deductions that are subtracted from gross wages prior to the calculation of the Federal, FICA and Medicare amounts. You can check which deductions are being subtracted by editing the Federal, FICA & Medicare deductions, clicking the E/D List in the lower right-hand corner and reviewing the deductions listed in the lower left-hand corner (Deductions Used To Compute This Deduction). From what I can see, the Quarterly Tax Report is listing exactly the same thing as the W-2 (because, again, there is no place on the W-2 for Gross Wages).

Susan Thomas] Explanation of W-2 Box 1 vs. Gross Wages The discrepancy between gross wages and W-2 Box 1 wages is normal and expected. Box 1 on the W-2 form shows ""Wages, tips, other compensation"" that are subject to Federal Income Tax withholding, not the employee's total gross wages. Various pre-tax deductions reduce the amount that appears in Box 1. Proposed Solution The difference of $20,582 between the gross wages and Box 1 wages represents pre-tax deductions that reduce the employee's taxable income for Federal Income Tax purposes. These could include retirement plan contributions, health insurance premiums, HSA contributions, and other pre-tax benefits. Steps to verify the W-2 information Check the Federal Income Tax deduction setup by going to Payroll > Maintenance > Employee > select the employee > go to the Deductions tab > select the Federal deduction. Click the ""E/D List"" button in the lower right-hand corner to view ""Deductions Used To Compute This Deduction"" in the lower left-hand corner. The deductions listed here are subtracted from gross wages before calculating Federal Income Tax, which explains why Box 1 shows a lower amount than gross wages. Verify that the same setup applies to FICA (Box 3) and Medicare (Box 5) deductions, as these may have different pre-tax treatments. Confirm that the W-2 Box 1 amount matches the ""Federal wages"" amount on the Quarterly Tax Report for the year, which would validate that the system is reporting consistently. Information to share with employees Box 1 on the W-2 shows taxable wages subject to Federal Income Tax, not their gross wages. The difference represents pre-tax deductions that benefit employees by reducing their taxable income. This is standard tax reporting practice and is working as designed. The previous year may have shown different amounts if the employee had different pre-tax deductions.

Tony Williams] Possible AI solution Issue Dori Huck from the City of Bridgeport needs to import new budget numbers for the 2025-2026 budget year and wants to confirm if this should be done before closing out the current fiscal year. Proposed Solution Based on the documentation and related tickets, Dori should import the new budget numbers BEFORE closing out the fiscal year. This is the correct process in PowerManager's General Ledger module. The system is designed to handle next year's budget while the current year is still active. Steps to complete Go to General Ledger > Budgeting > Export/Import Next Year Click the ""Import"" button to access the import functionality Select the prepared spreadsheet file containing the 2025-2026 budget figures Complete the import process following the on-screen prompts Verify the imported budget by going to General Ledger > Budgeting > Edit Amounts and clicking the ""Next"" button to view next year's budget Additional Information The documentation confirms that PowerManager is specifically designed to accommodate setting up next year's budget before closing the current fiscal year. When the fiscal year is eventually closed, the ""Next"" budget figures will automatically become the ""Current"" budget figures for the new fiscal year.
